I have another pair of Sandalistas that I love which is also quite snug around the toes and a bit short in the heel strap. Maybe I could go up a size but I won't for this pair because the straps bother the ball of my left foot. I returned and will look for another style of Sandalistas to try.
Got these for a weekend trip and they barely held up. For the cost, I expected way more durability, probably should've saved up for a canister instead.
Smells good, but it was broken and the glass is cracked bad.

I wanted to love this more than I did. The prose is undeniably gorgeous, and it's the kind of book that sticks with you after you've set it down. But after carrying it around for a couple weeks of on-and-off reading, the paperback binding started separating from the spine, which was disappointing. It's not a book you can toss in a bag without the cover getting pretty roughed up quickly. The ideas inside are solid and layered, but the physical copy just didn't hold up well to normal use.

Bought this for my daughter's diorama project. The sculpt is decent but the paint job is really sloppy, colors are dull and don't match the listing at all. She still uses it for imaginative play, so it's not a total loss. I'd only suggest it for kids who care more about play than realistic details.

This is a VERY LARGE umbrella. The colors are bright and because of the way it is vented the wind does not easily get up under it. The button to open it works easily and it closes as expected. It would be too expensive for my budget but is clearly and excellent umbrella.
Bought this for my dad who hates complicated gadgets, and he's used it daily for years without a single hiccup. Perfect for non-techy folks.
Finally found a can opener that opens cans simply and easy. What more can I say except that it's well made and sturdy? Nuff said.

I had gone through 2 "cheapie" scroll saws over the past several years. I do a fair amount of fret work and intarsia. I finally have a machine that holds the blade tightly and is easy to thread into pilot holes.the blower works great. Having the arm tilt is an invaluable feature for me. I am not crazy about the hold down...the arms are too far apart for some of the small work I do. But other than that, a fantastic machine, and getting the stand free is a great incentive. A built in light and blade holder would nice added features. I just completed cutting and building a very intricate clock that was all fret work with this saw. It's outstanding to operate. Changing the blade and re-tensioning from one hole to the next is fast and easy to do. I like the extra long air hose on the saw; it does a nice job of keeping the work area clear of dust when cutting and it adjusts easily. This saw also runs very smoothly and quite. If you enjoy doing fret work or making small detail cuts this is the saw to have.

I really enjoyed this book. It is a cliff hanger, so be warned of you really don't like that. I usually don't mind, but I kind of did with this one. Shortly after beginning the book, I knew she wasn't going to may the guy she was dating, but I thought she would marry another character in the book. That didn't happen, although I suspect she will in another book. One thing I really didn't like was at the end it announced that the guy she dated for most of the book was engaged, but it didn't day to whom (although I'm pretty sure whom). Still, it was a very enjoyable read. The characters were very likeable & the book was very clean.
A must-have for any gardener tired of dull pruners.
Got way more bricks than expected for the price, kids love it.

So far, so good. I've had it for a few months but have only used it a hand full of times. It works well and I haven't had any issues beside with the instructions: they weren't very clear. It appears as though it doesn't work, the first time you use it, unless you press down the button on the back for longer than a few seconds. Other than that, I've had no issues. It works well and a decent price.

Wood chips/curls are sharp and poke through the covering. Had to pick them out only to find more kept working their way to the surface. Dog wasn't able to use in this condition. Made in China.... Stuffing was brittle and course. Ended up emptying out stuffing and filling with store purchased fiber-fill. Very expensive project.
Lasted three weeks before the clamp completely snapped mid-ride. Total waste of money.
For the price, this little scale saved me from overweight bag fees. Totally worth it.

This is a birthday present for my 2 year old daughter so it's still in the package and we have not tried it on baby Stella yet but it looks adorable and we have been very happy with all the baby Stella items we have. I would love if the company would make matching jammies for little girls in the future.
Excellent product. Not real loud and very good quality. Does well for our Malti Poo. Gets hot FAST though

This is great for an GM running any form of RPG. Always nice to have extra dice for new players or to give away as rewards for players. Good variety of dice they are each individually packaged in a mini zip lock baggy and come in a big cloth material bag as pictured. The dice did not have any defects to note they are a bit smaller than chess ex dice I have but barely noticeable. They come in a variety colorS which may vary from the stock picture. I did not recieve any duplicate colors. I have used them many times already and would happily purchase them again. I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.
Good range and separation. All my devices run well and set up is easy, even if you go old school on it.
I tell all my red wine friends to grab these, they make every pour feel special.
Quality built. Handle can be detached from the hook driver. Hook driver can be used with a cordledd drill. Standard size screw driver bits will fit into handle. I'd buy it again.

My wife thinks I'm stupid because I have several flashlights that have multiple modes and whenever you turn them on it is usually in the wrong mode. Do you really think that you need a flashlight with a strobe? I bought her this simple on off light and she loves it. Truth be told, it does make a lot more sense than a five mode tactical light. Came with a good battery and a charger.

Bought this for my husband's gaming chair since the old one gave out completely. Within two weeks, the new cylinder started dropping about an inch every hour, which is honestly worse than the dead one it replaced. He's not a huge guy, maybe 180 pounds, so there's really no excuse. The install wasn't terrible, I'll give it that, but what's the point if it doesn't hold? I'd only recommend this to someone who enjoys slowly sinking while working or gaming and finds that entertaining. For anyone who actually wants to stay at desk height, hard pass. Total waste of money and time.
Too small for anything useful. I wanted to use one to cover the camera on my computers but they're too small! I'm sorry I bought them, but since they were so cheap, I won't bother to return them.
Put this in my old laptop six months ago and it's still running smooth without a single hiccup. Solid bang for the buck.

Got this test for my 6-month-old rescue pup because the shelter's best guess just didn't match what we were seeing every day. They said lab mix, maybe 45 pounds fully grown. Results came back and honestly, it made way more sense, mostly Catahoula, some boxer, and a splash of border collie. Explains her herding instinct and that wild energy level. The process was super simple, swab took maybe 30 seconds, and results arrived faster than expected. It's been a total game-changer for training and understanding her quirks. If you're even a little curious about your mutt's background, this thing is worth every penny. Already recommended it to three people at the dog park.

I'm a 38D and usually dread ordering bras online, but this one surprised me. The fabric is soft with just enough stretch to move with you, and the underwire doesn't dig in after a full workday. I'd read that the cups run slightly generous, so I stuck with my normal size and it's spot-on, no gaping, just a smooth shape under t-shirts. The band feels secure without riding up, and I'm not constantly adjusting the straps. I'd especially recommend this to anyone with a larger bust who wants everyday comfort without sacrificing support. It's become my go-to for long shifts and weekends alike. Honestly thinking about grabbing a second one in another color.
Great little product. Easy to use and no fuss. Furniture stays in place on tiled floors now!

This film does an incredible job of showing Patton's tactical brilliance without ever turning him into a simple hero. The opening speech alone is worth the price, Scott's delivery is so magnetic you forget you're watching an actor and not actual archival footage. I really appreciated how the movie didn't shy away from his ego or his flaws, especially the slapping incident, but still made you understand why his men respected him. The North Africa campaign sequences are staged beautifully, with wide shots that give a real sense of scale. It's rare to find a war biopic that balances battlefield strategy and personal complexity this well.

My order arrived two days early in a compact, well-padded box with zero damage. Opening it was a treat, the bags were neatly sealed and the bright instruction booklet felt crisp. First build snapped together satisfyingly quick on the kitchen table. Honestly, at this price point, it's an instant mood booster right out of the package.
Checks off all the boxes for a very inexpensive TV

Got it to work finally. Can't hear much if any difference. First tried it on cheap FM radio and all I got was static and hisses. Then tried it on Kindle Fire and could hear no difference. On mp3 player maybe some volume but hard to really tell. Bottom line I can not-repeat-not recommend this.

I wanted to like this frame for the price, but you get what you pay for. The plastic is flimsy and the backing doesn't stay put, so the photo slides right out. It's fine if you just need something super temporary, but for anything you actually care about displaying, it's not worth it. I ended up taping the back, which defeats the whole purpose of a frame.
This little story packs a punch. I especially loved how Fern's quiet confidence balances Carter's gruffness, made their chemistry feel real and earned. Perfect for a quick, satisfying escape.

I loved Beg for Mercy and Hide from Evil. This book is a part of a series. I was really excited this book came out. As soon as I saw it I grabbed it. This is Talia story. She is getting her life back together after she was tortured. She was in awful 'relationship' which is mentioned in Beg for Mercy. She was helped by Jack. I liked Talia the most part but she got stupid which I found annoying. Jack our hero is there to protect/help Talia (she doesn't know he was)had potential but he fell flat for me. This book talks about Gemini Security/Gemini Men. I felt like Alden was just trying to get me to buy those books. Not going to happen. This book seemed rush and didn't have the depth I felt in Beg for Mercy and Hide from Evil. Not a terrible book but I certainly won't be grabbing a book by Alden just b/c she wrote it. By the way, if you want to read this book I recommend you read Beg for Mercy 1st. It explains the back story which you really don't get in this book. If I didn't read the 1st 2 books in the series I think I would have given it 2 stars.
Great item. Doesn't work for all cats but that doesn't mean the product is bad. Some of my cats don't like catnip. Just know your cats.

I actually purchased these because of the lid and the infrared open. This was to keep my dog out of the garbage. Looks great. Opening works great. Easy to use. (On another note, it took less than two days for my very smart dog to realize she only had to put her very long snout over the red light to open the can!)
Arrived super fast, box was pristine, and my kid dove right in!
I was shocked by the quality for what I paid. Super soft, no break-in period, and they look way more expensive than they are.
I expected a basic ball, but the bounce is wildly unpredictable and keeps my cat leaping. Way more entertaining than her usual toys, total win for the price.

The box showed up looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, corners crushed, inner tray cracked, and the product itself was just rattling around loose inside with zero padding. First impression out of the box was honestly pretty sad. The plastic felt cheap and greasy, and there was a weird scuff on the side like it had already been handled a bunch. I figured maybe it's just cosmetic, but the battery door didn't even click shut properly, had to wiggle it a few times just to get it seated. For something brand new, it already felt used and beat up. Definitely not the fresh, solid piece of gear I was expecting for the price. Huge letdown right from unboxing.
The stand is perfect for raising the TV enough to be able to place the Blue-Ray player underneath. The setup looks nicely organized and neat.

I've taken this camera to a few weekend soccer games now and it's held up nicely. A few minor scuffs but zero performance issues. For casual action shots, it still feels solid and dependable after a month.

I'll be honest, the thickness had me side-eyeing my nightstand for a second, but once I cracked it open I completely forgot. It reads fast, and I kept sneaking chapters during lunch. The real draw is how grounded the three brothers feel, their dynamic makes the bigger fantasy stakes hit harder. I'd hand this to anyone who loved The Name of the Wind but wished it had more family banter, or to readers who think they don't have time for epic fantasy. It's a chunky book that doesn't feel like one.

We bought this thinking it was a good price but really for the same amount, you could buy materials for many more bracelets. This one only lets you make the 5 or 6 that you see. We bought this for a 9.5 year old. She couldn't really understand the directions. I read them and once I got the knack of it tried to teach her, but she couldn't do it. In the end I had to make them myself and give the bracelet to the original gift recipient. Thus my thinking that it would have been cheaper to buy the materials and make the bracelets the gift. I will say, we didn't know the girl well ahead of time, so we weren't sure of her crafting level. At 10, friendship bracelets were all the rage in my school, and all the girls were making them on the playground or between breaks. But not every kid is adept or interested. Additionally, for just a small bit more you can buy a kit from the same company which contains many more materials. Skip this kit.
This shrug was exactly what I needed for my sleeveless jumpsuit. The sheer fabric kept me cool at an outdoor wedding and didn't snag once.

For under three bucks, this cozy Christmas read was a total steal. The small-town setting felt authentic, and the slow-burn romance was sweet without being cheesy. A few scenes felt a tad rushed, but overall it's a warm, feel-good story that's well worth the pocket change.
Nice clean bright light one out of the box that I received does have a flicker

This little table set is a steal for what you pay. My toddler drags it all over the kitchen and it's held up pretty well to snacks, toys, and general chaos. The chairs are lightweight but sturdy enough for a wiggly kid, and assembly took maybe ten minutes. The drawing surface is more of a foam-like top, so don't expect a true whiteboard feel, it does get tiny dents from pressing too hard and some marker colors leave faint shadows even after wiping. We just threw a cheap dry erase board on top and called it a day. For the price, it's a solid buy for meals and playtime, just manage expectations on the art side.

This wheel is huge compared to the tiny disc my dwarf hamster had before. The cork lining is great for traction and she took to it immediately. My only minor gripe is that the metal stand slides a bit on the glass tank floor, so I had to wedge it between hides. Other than that, it's dead quiet and she uses it all night.

I couldn't put this down once the hidden family secret started unravelling. The tension between loyalty and truth kept me flipping pages way past midnight. The coastal setting felt so vivid I could practically smell the salt air, and the bittersweet ending left me happily wrecked.
I'm using one to operate both garage doors from a Rasberry pi. They work perfectly.

CHicken Fajita is one of my favorite mountain house flavors. My rule with all mountain house is that I make sure the water is super hot and I don't put the recommended amount in. I also leave it in the bag lnger than recommended.

I have been buying Jungle Mocs for my wife for years and finally said, what the hey, I should try them myself and so I bought them and now understand why my wife and everyone loves them. Give your tired feet a present of a snuggly yet supportive shoe you can wear anywhere.

I loved this DVD!!! But, it made me really sad because the series is gone!! After it was over, I wanted to see more!!It was great to see the unusual suspects, but I have to confess I missed Colonol O'Neill, I wish they could have at least had him do a cameo appearance. It was nice to wrap up the Ori story line, we were all left hanging abit with the last televised episode. But, they left a little mystery behind, because we still had to wonder if they were really finished!!! This is a DVD that all fans of the series must own!!
Had this Blu-ray for a couple years now and it still plays perfectly without a single skip.

I've used this for late-night repairs in tight spots, and it's a solid helper. The beam is crisp, and it sits nicely on my head. Only gripe is the battery life dips faster than I'd like during extended projects.

Our collie mix has been absolutely obsessed with this ball for about three months now. She slams it into fence posts, rocks, and the concrete patio at full speed, and honestly I expected it to crack within a week. There are plenty of scuffs and scratches, but no splits or punctures whatsoever. The hard plastic shell holds up way better than I anticipated. Only minor complaint is it's a little noisy on pavement, but that's just part of the chaos. She pushes it with her nose for ages and comes inside completely wiped out. Great buy if you need something sturdy for a relentless herder.

The adjustable color temperature is a standout, shifting from warm to cool light is seamless and really helps with eye strain. The base is a bit heavier than expected, which limits placement options, but overall it's a solid, stylish lamp.
For the price, it's a fun little hideout. Just needs a bit of DIY tweaking to keep it standing.

For around twenty bucks, this bit set is a steal. You get all the common sizes in a solid little case that doesn't hog drawer space. I've used mine on everything from furniture assembly to light automotive work, and they still look new. Hard to beat for the price.

It's an okay take on a topical issue, but it's not particularly novel. Books on power and change by, for example, Dacher Keltner, John Kotter, and Robert Cialdini have already made many of the key points here. Sure, the examples are fresher (e.g., #MeToo and Trump), but those have also been covered in recent and more focused books that delve deeper into the details.

We have a brave little dog that is fearless of most anything. However, at the distant sound of thunder he begins to shake. During a lightening storm he always has problems with nervousness. We live in Louisiana so thunder storms are common. The July 4th fireworks celebrations also causes him a lot of anxiety. The Thunderchews have worked great on him. Within minutes it calms him down. I didn't think it would work that well but am so happy that it does. Our dog was pitiful during thunder episodes. I am not sure if it works the same on all dogs but I can testify that for my dog it is a miracle and has a great calming effect. I will purchase this again when my supply runs out and would recommend it for anyone with a dog who has anxiety with loud noises.
this bota bag is awsome, perfect size, strong strap, really well made. a true spanish product, (no chinese knock off here). easy to fill, and drink from, water or wine. a great buy.

Perfect for my craft booth setup. I use it to show off resin keychains and small sculptures. Assembly was straightforward, just take your time. The glass panels make everything pop even without lights. I'd recommend it to anyone doing markets or pop-up shops on a budget. Not heirloom quality, but looks way more expensive than it is.

I've worn and washed this several times now and it still looks brand new. No fading, no loose threads, and the shape holds perfectly. Honestly surprised by how well it's held up, feels like it'll last ages.

My ASUS Transporter was basically a paperweight after the stock cable frayed. This replacement snapped right in and charges fast. If you're clinging to an older tablet, grab this, it's a total lifesaver. I'd recommend it to anyone nursing aging tech back to life.
The box arrived crushed, and the item was just wrapped in a thin plastic bag. Looked nice at first glance, but a small scuff was already there.
Got this for my 6-year-old and she won't take it off, the little duck beak on the hood totally makes it. Stays put even when she's splashing in puddles.

I grabbed this on a whim and ended up finishing it in two sittings. The story held up nicely on a re-read a few weeks later, which says something about its staying power. The pacing felt even smoother the second time around, and little details I missed before popped out. Solid Kindle formatting too, no weird glitches or formatting hiccups after lending it to my sister. It's holding up well.
These are some of my dogs favorite treats. If they had a choice they would get them all the time if they could.

This thing cost way too much for what you actually get. I'd been dealing with neck pain and figured splurging on a premium pillow would be worth it, but nope. It's basically a fancy sack of shredded foam that goes completely flat within an hour. I kept waking up with my head basically on the mattress and my neck even worse than before. Fluffing it up every night got old real fast. For this price I expected something that would hold its shape for more than a week. My old twenty-dollar pillow from Target performs better, honestly. Total waste of money and I'm past the return window so now I'm just annoyed every time I see it on the bed.
This was my son's favorite Christmas present this year. He loves the music, lights and movement. He is three.
Super happy with these. They fit perfectly and feel soft all day long.
I buy these books for my son.. He loves them all...

This book is a steal for the price. I'm learning Spanish casually and the clear, everyday photos with side-by-side terms make it stick without feeling like a chore. The paper quality is surprisingly nice for what I paid. Perfect for building practical vocabulary on a budget.

Phenomenal read... well written... Shows that there are lessons for all to learn; even when you think you are the helper/teacher/caregiver, you can always learn something and come out the other side a better person. Thought provoking book that likely aligns very tightly with the lives of many who deal with paralyzing injuries.

I was honestly a little skeptical this would make much difference in our basement laundry nook, it's one of those corners that always felt damp and stale no matter how much I aired it out. Set it up about ten days ago and kept the door mostly shut. The improvement isn't magical overnight, but the musty edge is about 80% gone now, which is better than I expected for the price. It's compact, totally silent, and I forget it's there until I go in and notice the air just feels lighter. Took off one star only because I wish the cord were a few inches longer for placement flexibility. Still, solid buy.
Quite pricey but the quality of the sole is excellent! lasts long and it also comes with a lifetime warranty from sperry!

I grabbed this for day trips when I don't want to haul a full kit, and it's been perfect. The build feels really solid, and the zippers glide smoothly. I can fit my mirrorless body with a prime attached plus a second lens, batteries, and a few filters without it getting bulky. The padding is thick enough that I don't worry about bumps. I'd recommend it to hobbyist shooters who prioritize mobility over carrying a ton of gear.
Excellent unit. Much better than the original. Charges quickly and efficiently. Glad I found this unit online. So far so good
I use this to carry everything from drill bits to tiny washers in a tool bag and it holds them all tightly inside within their respective compartments. Recommmend.

Box arrived crushed on one side, and when I opened it, the opener was just sliding around loose in a flimsy plastic tray, no foam, no real padding. Right away I noticed scratches all over the top and a weird rattle inside. The whole thing felt lightweight and cheap, nothing like the photos online. I didn't even get to the point of testing it on a bottle because the foil cutter section looked crooked and barely attached. For something supposed to feel a little premium, the packaging and initial quality scream dollar-store knockoff. Honestly, I'm not even curious enough to try it, just gonna return it and find something sturdier.
Came super fast in a sturdy box, no dents at all. Looks cute and feels solid right out of the package.
Honestly, I didn't expect much for the price, but this drip pan is solid. It caught a slow leak I had missed for weeks and saved my laundry room floor. Fits perfectly under my washer.

Love this harness. We have 3 bigs dogs that we walk regularly and before we switched to this harness and Halti double leads they pulled us around the block. We actually use the leads as "anchors". We strap the lead around our waist and connect the other end to the front clip of each dog's harness. We then use our old regular leads to clip to the harness right between the shoulder blades. I can't say they don't pull at all with these harnesses but it has decreased significantly. (It's our own fault because we haven't properly trained them to not pull, but who has time for that). Using the leads like this also gives us a much more control over them. It used to feel like they would pull my arm out of my socket but now when they do pull (if they see a cat or squirrel and they try to lunge) they are trying to pull against my whole body weight at my center of gravity and get no where fast. Definitely recommend this harness and the Halti double lead.

The book is for an Art History class I am taking for self-improvement. It is in great 'used' condition; I did not want to pay the price for a new one. Thanks, amazon, for making it available to me. The art work in this book is what I will be seeing when I go to Paris & London next month!

The padded envelope arrived in two days with zero damage, corners were crisp, no bent pages. As soon as I unwrapped it, the glossy cover just pulled me in. I read the whole thing in one sitting, completely hooked from that very first masked encounter. Such a fun, spicy start to the series.

My copy arrived a day early and was packed really securely, no dings, no bent corners, just pristine. I barely glanced at the first page to get a feel for the writing style and ended up losing twenty minutes before I even set it down. The cover has a nice matte finish too, which was a pleasant surprise. Super happy with this purchase already.

I've had this on my keychain for about three weeks now and the plastic casing is already cracking near the split ring attachment point. Light output wasn't great to begin with, it's dim and bluish, barely enough to find a keyhole in total darkness, but now the switch is getting flaky too. Half the time I have to press it three or four times before it flickers on, and sometimes it just stays off. I didn't expect a $10 mini light to be built like a tank, but falling apart after light pocket duty is disappointing. The only reason I'm not giving it one star is that the magnet on the back still holds okay on my fridge, so it's not totally useless yet.
Love these bottles. Had a problem one was broken and the seller was easy to deal and replaced the broken bottle no problem. I would definitely buy again
The twist in chapter twelve caught me completely off guard, loved it.

These Lowas replaced a pair of Lowa hiking boots I had and loved for almost 15 years. The fit is perfect for my feet,roomy in the front,as hiking boots should be,without being too big. The prune color is just beautiful. Also I am able to wear my orthotics in them comfortably. I look forward to years of happy hiking feet.
intermittent and non-cohesive storyline, an overall impression of a story put together by cut-and-paste for lack of attention to the plot itself
Perfect fit. Will buy more. This only comes with the paddle.

It another one of those movies where twins are played by the same actor. This tired old trick was cute years ago with the Parent Trap. But now it's dull now matter what type of plot they try. This one is about a pot grower and a school teacher. Maybe this might of been interesting if they had used real twins as the actors. Instead Edward Norton does a poor job of switching accents and pretending to look at himself as a twin. This very old visual trick never works. You can tell when people do not make real eye contact with each other. This film might have even been interesting if they had used two different actors who look kind of alike. But overall it's not funny or full of action like Pinapple Express. Don't waste your time.
If you are familiar with the original Brady Bunch this movie is very funny. I docked it a star for innuendo but all in all a good movie.
although not as good as "my truck is stuck", my son enjoyed this book too.

I love this bag! It's large, long, and sturdy! The zipper is great as well, as most similar bags don't have one. Also, the color / pattern variation is random, I know, but I got just what I had hoped for!
These little guys were a total hit at my kid's party, and for the cost I honestly can't complain.

For eight bucks, this is a steal. Bubba's twisted sense of justice had me chuckling more than I expected, and the pacing rarely drags. A few side characters felt thin, but the main crew delivers plenty of grit and laughs for the money.
The box arrived smushed but the ring was okay, just not quite as rustic as I hoped.

I really wanted to love this thing. It was accurate for the first month or so, then the display started flickering and finally died mid-cook. Pretty frustrating when you're trying to nail a brisket. Can't trust it to last more than a few weeks.

Got my Healthy Human bottle two days early, always a nice surprise. It arrived in minimal, recyclable packaging with zero plastic, which I really appreciated. The bottle itself was wrapped snugly in a slim box, no scuffs or dings. First thing I noticed was how solid the stainless steel feels in hand; the powder coat is smooth and grippy, not slick. Popped the lid off and gave it a sniff, no weird metallic or factory smell, just clean. I filled it with ice water around noon and by bedtime it was still chilly, with ice cubes mostly intact. The mouth opening is wide enough for regular ice cubes too. Overall, unboxing felt thoughtful and the build quality stood out immediately. Super happy with it.
It is a very classy set! Just like the picture. I love that it's machine washable, though haven't washed it yet. The comforter is not too heavy and thick.
The box looked a bit rough on arrival but everything inside was intact, so no real complaints from me.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up loving every page. Austen's wit is so sharp here, she gently pokes fun at the gothic novels I used to devour, but never in a mean-spirited way. Catherine is wonderfully relatable, stumbling through social situations and letting her imagination run wild, and her growth feels genuine and earned. I'd especially recommend this to anyone who grew up on mystery or suspense stories and later realized real life rarely follows those dramatic scripts. It's also perfect for fans of clever, character-driven humor who want something light but not shallow. Honestly, it's a cozy, smart read that left me smiling.
Not as sticky as other brands, but otherwise are perfect. Nice to wear under a particularly thin bralette.
Classic movie that I was thrilled to find on DVD. I was a little worried about quality since it is an old movie but it was a great picture.

I'll admit I was a little skeptical before this arrived, could a slicer really be that much better than a knife and some patience? Turns out, yes. The first time I ran a cucumber through it and got perfectly even, paper-thin slices in seconds, I just stood there grinning. Cleanup is way simpler than I expected; nothing gets trapped in weird crevices. It's compact enough to live in a drawer without taking over, but sturdy enough that I'm not worried about it slipping mid-slice. For salads, gratins, or just making prep less of a chore, it's been a quiet workhorse. Exceeded my hopes by a long shot.

The workmanship of this garment seems fine, and if it fit right, I'd be pretty excited about it. I figured it'd be a little loose but the size was on ultra-sale, so I decided to try it. Turns out their plus sizes are REALLY plus-sized. It's very pretty, and I wish it fit right.
This was another great story in this series. I cannot wait for the next book. If you like strong female characters and their alpha male loves you will enjoy this series.

I was looking for a small, oblong shaped gold laced edge doily for my kitchen table. The table seats four and this size fits perfectly. I wanted a piece much shorter than a table runner. I placed a small centerpiece on it and I got the dimensional look I was hoping to achieve. The fabric and lace looks much better than I had hoped. It is not flimsy. Haven't washed it yet, but I feel it will require a light pressing which is okay. Package arrived early and well packaged. Glad I purchased it. Exactly what I needed.

I've got a little terrier mix who usually shreds anything plush within a day, so I didn't have high hopes for this duck. To my surprise, it held up through weeks of tug-of-war sessions and being thrashed around the living room. The stitching around the neck finally started to give after a couple months, but honestly, that's a record in our house. The material feels sturdy without being rough, and it didn't leave fluff everywhere when it did eventually tear. We got way more mileage out of it than expected. I'd definitely buy another one for her, even knowing it's not indestructible.
My pups go nuts for these, and they hold up great in their bowls or as a quick reward.

This thing is a beast for lighting up my whole driveway. I've had it up for a week and honestly it's almost too bright, it washes out the entire front yard like a mini stadium. Detection range is solid; it catches me walking back from the mailbox no problem. Install wasn't terrible but the bracket feels a little flimsy and getting the fixture seated right took some patience and a few choice words. Once it's locked in though it stays put. Overall I'm happy with it. For the price you're getting a crazy amount of light and reliable motion sensing, just be ready for a slightly annoying setup.
This thing glides across my tile so effortlessly, and it gets every last speck of dust. So happy I bought it.
My kids LOVE this. The torch is bright. The diamond block is just cool.
After six months and three re-reads, this paperback's spine is still tight and the cover hasn't curled. Holds up like a champ.
These are my go-to casual sneakers now. They look great and feel mostly comfy, just a tiny bit roomy in the heel.

This crate has been great for our 3 month old Corgi. He takes up about one-third to one-half of the crate right now, so we have been using the divider. It's great that we will still be able to use this crate when he is full size.

I'd only recommend this if you're totally fine with a teaser that stops just when it gets interesting. The writing's decent, but the characters barely have room to breathe. For the price, I expected a full story, not what feels like a sample. Maybe grab it on sale if you're curious.

Very pleased....I have been using the straw Camelbak for the last 7 years and the last couple I bought broke in some way or another or leaked so I switched to this one and just received it a few days ago and I like it a lot. Very sturdy, does not leak, keeps water cold

I'm pretty impressed with this litter trap mat. The material is spongy and soft for the cat to walk on and it's big enough to cover the entrance to our litter box while still providing a lot of cover on either side and in front so the cat can't just jump over it on the way out. The spacing of the material is wide enough so that it really can be vacuumed and shaken out (the previous litter trap mat we had was made from a similar material but once litter got in there it was very hard to get out even after vigorous shaking or vacuuming). It's also a little thicker so that it doesn't feel flimsy. I've had some half-circle litter trap mats and they seemed like a good idea but left too much space on the sides of the entrance. This one covers everything. I really like this thing. Some reviewers mentioned a strong plastic smell when opening this and that's true but it's not a big deal. It's a mat that sits outside your cat's litter box, so plastic odor is not a big deal. It's definitely not perfect and doesn't catch all litter, but with good positioning it captures most and that perfect product that does it all hasn't been invented yet.
Book arrived two days early in perfect, bubble-wrapped condition. The cover design grabbed me instantly and I couldn't put it down once I started.
they are simple, sharp, and cut nicely. happy with them.

I first saw this version of "Ah! My Goddess" online via another legal video streaming service and I fell in love with it. I'm a huge fan of the OVA and this was done by the same director/producer Hiraoki Goda. This anime is excellent for those who are either new to Anime or for those who like Fantasy and "Slice of life" anime. It's characters all have great stories and the overall story arc reaches a nice climax at the last show of the first season, and finishes in the first episode(s) of the 2nd season. Great good guys, some moronic fall-back characters, wonderful bad guys (not very dangerous overall) and great mix of romance and missed opportunities by both of the protagonists.

This silver Moda Di Raza tie looks great. I've always wanted a silver neck tie, and this one is perfect. It looks great for more formal business meetings, and when meeting with clients. I am impressed with the quality of the tie; you don't need to spend a lot of money to get great durability.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up reading it in one sitting. The New Zealand setting felt vivid and refreshing, and I appreciated how the author handled the emotional fallout between Dion and Megan. Some of the dialogue felt a little rushed toward the end, but the chemistry between the leads kept me turning pages. A heartfelt, easy read.

My first Hakko was dead on arrival. When I turned it on, I got the error "h-E". After a lot of digging online, I found an obscure reference to that message pertaining to the failure of the heating element. It didn't seem very common, so I returned the iron and got a replacement. The replacement worked fine with very intermittent usage over the course of a few months. However, I needed to use the iron for a remote task recently. I put it in my toolbox along with some other tools. Upon reaching my destination and firing up the iron, I got the "h-E" message again! I was left without the main tool I needed for the task on that Sunday. Left with few options, I went to Home Depot and picked up a cheap Weller. Lo and behold, the Weller got the job done, and the Hakko is out of commission again. During the ~5 times that I was able to use the Hakko, I enjoyed its functionality. It just appears very delicate in my case. I wanted to love this well-respected iron, but I'm left with a dud yet again.
For what this cost, I expected at least a hint of color. It's basically a dull gray pebble. Total waste of money.

I drive a lot for work, so I finally upgraded to the Escort and I'm honestly mad I waited this long. The difference from my old detector is night and day, way fewer annoying false alerts from automatic doors and blind spot systems. The ramp-up is really smooth, so you're not jumping out of your skin every time it goes off. The smart cord is a small thing, but having the mute button right there is super convenient. I'd recommend this to anyone who commutes daily or does a ton of highway miles and just wants something that works without constant noise. Seriously, if you're on the fence, just get it.
Decent for the cost, but nothing to get excited about.

this is by far the creepiest dog toy ive ever had. and its really hard plastic. so not only does it make a ton of noise when it rolls around or is thrown but the sound effects are CREEPY! and it will randomly go off when u walk in the room or near the dog toy box. so imagine u are still half asleep, its dark out and im going to go feed the dogs, i reach in the dog food container and bump it and then i hear a creepy voice say something weird out of no where. do not buy unless you are getting it for someone else lol!
For the price, I expected at least a decent story, but this was a total waste of money and time.
I enjoyed going through the 31 Bible verses in different translations. I will b going through this book again and again!

Easy to read, This is a perfect book to read before reading the Mists of Avalon. I am a great fan of Ms Bradley, and an glad that she left us such great legacy. So far I have not read one of her books that I was bored with, or skipped ahead to read the ending. She kept me caught up in the moment. I did not want to miss a word
It's a decent pick if you just want a quick, no-pressure read before bed.
Thought I was getting a fun stamp set but the impressions are so faint you can barely tell what the design is. My kid was super disappointed. Cute stamps, but totally useless out of the box.

I mainly got this because I needed something to be able to watch the movies that I like when the main t.v. and computer are both being used by others. I can put my earphones in, sit back at the table and watch my movies to my hearts desire, not disturbing anyone else or them disturbing me. I love the fact that I could get it in pink...keeps the menfolk away from MY little DVD player..lol I see nothing wrong with the picture or sound quality, it's all alright with me!

These little bugs have the perfect crinkle wings, my son is obsessed with the sound. The soft antenna makes a great teether, and the clip actually stays on the stroller. Washed it a dozen times and the colors still look brand new.

I've watched this twice now and it holds up surprisingly well. The disc didn't skip or freeze at all, even after my kids got their sticky hands on it. The chariot sequence still gets me every time. A solid pick for a lazy Sunday rewatch that doesn't lose its charm.

This novel takes a look at a mystery, a Judge who disappeared without a trace in 1930, in New York. It was a case that garnered much attention at the time. The story is told from the viewpoints of Judge Crater's wife, mistress and household maid, all important women at his life at the time of the disappearance. The historical accuracy seems to be there, although I was disappointed that it wasn't shared in more detail. Prohibition, Tammany Hall, political corruption-so much going on then that could have added even more background to the story. The characters of the women in his life are wonderful, fully drawn and seeming true to life, and sometimes bigger than life. I was enthralled by the story, and hated to see it end.

I tore through this in one sitting. Ishmael's voice grabbed me from page one, sharp, weary, and unexpectedly tender beneath all the grit. The pacing never lets up, and the turn toward gardening as a kind of redemption really stuck with me. Brutal moments hit hard, but they feel earned. Perfect if you need a short, intense read that lingers.

Another reviewer labeled this book cute. Perfectly described this book. It's like the author wrote down all the awh moments and strung them together and made a book. There is no irony, tension or questions that you don't know the answers to. It is a pleasant read. And I certainly didn't have to put my finger over any word. A predictable chick lit read.
For under ten bucks, this Belkin case is a steal. It props my Kindle up perfectly for hands-free Netflix binges and feels sturdy enough to last. Total no-brainer purchase.
Worn it daily for months, still looks brand new.
PICTURE INCLUDED: the perfect size medkit. not to big and not too small. Great for hikes and EDC (every day carry)
Not even close to her usual heart-pounding suspense and character depth.
Bought it on a whim and it stuck with me for weeks, totally worth the few bucks.

Mall Rats may not be Oscar-worthy per se, but its till worth watching if you're a Kevin Smith fan. The film stars both Ben Affleck and former skateboarder Jason Lee in their film debuts. The plot: T.S. Quint (played by Jeremy London) and Brodie Bruce (played by the aforementioned Jason Lee) are two best friends, who spend too much time at their local mall, and are then dumped by their girlfriends Brandi Svenning (played by Claire Forlani) and Rene Mosier (played by Shannon Doherty) for being unemployed, immature and overall slackers. TS wants to get married to Brandi, even though he's still in college. But what stands in not only his way, but Brodie's way as well, is Brandi's father, Jared (played by Michael Rooker, of AMC's Walking Dead fame), who is hosting a game show, Truth or Date, at the duo's favorite mall. The two then "hire" the famed Clerks duo, Jay (Jason Mewes) and Silent Bob (film's director, Kevin Smith) to not only help them get their girlfriends back, but to trash the set of said show and get it kicked off the airwaves before it premieres. In an interesting subplot, Willam Black (played by Ethan Suplee in his film debut) has been spending his spare time from opening 'til closing staring at a Magic Eye poster, which is said to be a sailboat, however, he can't see it! The DVD: This DVD is awesome, it is double sided. It has the original theatrical version on Side A, and on Side B, an extended, unrated version of the film which runs approximately 2 hrs., 1 min. (121 min.) as opposed to the original theatrical version which runs 1 hr. 35 min. (95 min.) Aside from that, the extras are fantastic! A must buy if you're a Kevin Smith fan!!

Adorable toy. Fun and entertaining for young girls. Or boys. Well made and slightly interactive. The doll has movable arms and legs. It is cute and colorful. As cute as this is, though, I'm not sure I would want to pay the current price of $39.99 for it. I think that is way too much for this toy. Also, the packaging was a nightmare. I understand the need to protect it during shipping, but this was a bit overkill. My granddaughter loves it which is the only reason this gets 4 stars instead of 3.
For the price, this thing held up way better than I expected and the kids had a blast.

I'm honestly baffled by this bed. Within ten minutes of putting it down, both my pups started shredding it like it was a stuffed toy. The cover material seems to trigger some kind of prey drive, I've never seen them act this way with any other bed. Stuffing was everywhere in under an hour. I'd only recommend this to someone who wants an expensive, single-use destructible toy for their dogs, not an actual place to sleep. Total waste of money.

Update: July 11, 2014 I hade been giving the Princess Pia (my aging Siamese cat) other products to help ease her arthritic joints, asd could not see any improvement. Then I tried Nutramax Cosequin. But when I tried mixing Nutramax into her food, she wouldn't eat her food. I can't think WHY I keep thinking my (overweight) cat will starve, but I finally overcame my fear and kept putting it into her food - and she finally ATE it! Well, to keep this short, She now gets it twice daily and she eats it every time. She also runs, jumps, climbs, and chases a little "critter" that hangs on a long string from a helium balloon (her current favorite toy). She hadn't been doing any of those things, and had a bad limp. Now she's very active and her limp is barely perceptible. I'm ordering it again.

I picked this up on a whim during a sale and honestly didn't expect much for the price, but wow, this story totally hooked me. The blend of Native American lore and supernatural elements felt fresh without being gimmicky, and Dean's journey from ordinary kid to something more kept me flipping pages late into the night. Carey builds atmosphere really well, and the desert setting practically breathes off the screen. For a few bucks, you're getting solid world-building, genuine tension, and characters that actually grow on you. It's rare to find an indie title that punches this far above its price point. If you're into coming-of-age stories with a mystical edge, grab it, total steal.

Bass is muddy but acceptable in a speaker set in this price range. If you can recall to take extreme care when assembling, disassembling, or moving this speaker set, and if you're not looking for high fidelity, this is probably a perfectly fine purchase. My wife moved the woofer to make room while vacuuming and bumped the back of the woofer against the wall. This broke the tweeter audio connector internally, making it so that I had to wiggle the tweeter connector cable in order to make a connection - and high volumes would shake it loose almost immediately. Being only invested for $40 and being fairly certain the connector was probably wired separately from the circuit board, I decided to disassemble the woofer and attempt a repair. I quickly discovered that every seam and component are glued into place - even those which are (also) screwed-down. I also then discovered that the audio connector is part of the circuit board rather than being a separate lead-out connector. The repair was beyond my soldering skills and though I could have taken it to a repair shop, the cost of doing so would have exceeded the purchase price of a new speaker set.

Starts slowly, and you get that feeling like you read it before. It gets more tense as you turn each page. You will not be able to stop reading. You won't eat! You won't sleep! You will call in sick at work! You have to see what happens next! Whew. I finally caught my breath. I loved the book. It did what a good story should do; capture me, and raise my blood pressure to a dangerous number.

Jessica and Simon are recalled to Mogo for training, meanwhile, Volthoom, the first wielder of a Power Ring is back controlling of the guardian Rami for his own ends. Simon and Jessica's training dominates the first three issues and these are interesting bits. It's great to see them interact with the rest of the Green Lantern Universe and the characters who are traditionally associated with the Green Lanterns. Volthoom's story continues in the background in the first two issues and then comes to the foreground in penultimate issue. This is an epic story that goes back to the founding of the Lanterns. Writer Sam Humphries has done a great job slowly teasing this out and there are some key revelations in this issue that set up Humphries' final collection. The art is great as well. I particularly appreciate the versatility of the artist as they are not only able to beautifully render known characters but also species we haven't seen before. Overall, this is another great installment of a superb Green Lantern series.

I was really hoping this would work for my morning jogs, but the case barely grips the device. It popped off twice just from light movement. I'd only recommend it if you're sitting at a desk and never touching the screen much, the protector makes swiping a chore. For anyone even mildly active, skip this. It looks nice, but that's about all it has going for it.

Wow Allie Juliette Mousseau has done an amazing job again. This book can be read as a stand-alone! At times this book brought me to my knees because I could feel the pain of Liam and Quinn. These two have been dealt a s*** hand. The book does flip from past to present a little more than my liking but you do need the back history for their story to make sense. Quinn is a kid that has a mother who doesn't want her, she throws her out at 15. She has to learn how to survive on the streets. Being a 15 year old young lady on the streets is dangerous. And living in the wrong part of town you only have a few options in survival, drugs to numb the pain or selling yourself to devil to have some money. She makes her choice and it is neither. She meets Liam and he is living the same life. No parents, the abuse can be to much when you go from foster home to foster home. Liam learns to fight to survive. And when he meets Quinn he fights enough for both of them. That is so much for anyone to handle but they both do they best they can with what they have. Now go forward 10 years, their demons are still with them. They carry them everyday but Quinn did what anyone at young age would do when her world crashes, she ran as far away as possible leaving Liam behind. Liam did what he did and does best he fights. MMA fighting. But what brings Quinn home brings them face to face again. The question is, is it to little to late or will they save each other once and for all? I would have to say if you have any problems with violence both physical and sexual this book could trigger your own dreams, but that being said I would recommend this book to others and I for one can not wait to read more about these brothers!!

Arrived in sturdy, no-fuss packaging, no weird chemical smell right out of the box. Unrolled flat within minutes and felt instantly secure underfoot. The texture is grippy but soft, and it looks way nicer in person than I expected. Really impressed so far.

I first viewed this receiver at Best Buy and after thinking about it I ordered it off Amazon. Free shipping and a slightly lower price. Well worth the money. It produces wonderful sound. I've been using it mostly with my blue ray player and it really rocks.
Bought for my summer geography class. Great book really goes into detail. Was much more helpful that professor.

I grew up on Kinsey Millhone, and reaching Y felt like closing a chapter of my own life. There's something so comforting about sliding back into her world, the no-nonsense attitude, the quiet Santa Teresa streets, the way she pours herself a glass of wine after a long day of chasing down leads. This one hits all the right notes: tight pacing, sharp dialogue, and that bittersweet undertone knowing it's the last full case we'll get from Sue. I've read every single entry over the years, and this one still surprised me. It's not just a mystery; it's a farewell to an old friend. If you've been along for the ride, this one sticks with you long after the last page.

I went into Majority Rules expecting it to be a little chaotic, and it absolutely delivered on that front. The whole idea of arguing why Darth Vader would make the best brunch companion is exactly the kind of low-stakes ridiculousness I was hoping for. Setup was a breeze, and we jumped right in without anyone checking their phone for rules clarifications. The only reason I'm not giving it five stars is that the novelty can fade a bit once you've cycled through the question cards a couple times with the same group. Still, for a spontaneous game night where you want laughter more than strategy, it hits the mark. Definitely keeping it in the rotation for when we have guests over.

Started at the beginning of the Alex Cross series and I can't wait for the next book as I get through each. I'm glad that he has other series I can read once I finish the Cross books. Thank you for writing in the way that keeps me wanting more!

I wanted to love these bulbs since they cut my kitchen's energy use noticeably. The light temperature is perfect, not that harsh blue-white you get with some LEDs. Unfortunately, after just three months, one started flickering like a strobe light and another completely died. Swapping them out this often defeats the purpose. Great concept, poor longevity.

This is a great mixture of flavors for a dog. My dog absolutely loves these. I have several brands of treats here right now but she loves these best of all of them. They are soft bites yet they are larger and a little thicker than some of the other brands. I'm haapy with the ingredients and they don;t upset her stomach which is what I like best. Very happy with these.

We've had this little harness for about four months now, and it's held up amazingly through daily walks, mud puddles, and a few good tugs at squirrels. Stitching is still solid, clips work smoothly, and the fabric hasn't frayed at all. Really impressed with how tough it's been for such a lightweight design.

For the price of a single title, you're getting three solid cowboy romances by Linda Lael Miller. Each story stands strong, and together they're a steal. I've already burned through the rest of the series because her writing just hooks you. Perfect for anyone who loves western love stories on a budget.

Nalini Singh certainly writes these books at a good pace. This is the fifth in the series, following 'Slave to Sensation' (September 2006), 'Visions Of Heat' (March 2007), 'Caressed By Ice' (September 07) and 'Mine To Possess' (February 2008), so she's managing to churn out two per year. I suppose this is on a par with many other authors but, having finished 'Hostage to Pleasure', I wonder if this author with her original ideas is perhaps a slight victim of her success. 'Hostage to Pleasure' was a good read. We had a varied cast of characters as always (shapeshifters including leopards, wolves and even rats), the Psy (Mr Spock-like beings who communicate telepathically) and occasional references to humans. We have characters who have their own bad memories, demons, difficulties, fears and a central love story which shows two people growing together and learning to gain strength from each other. Unfortunately for this reader at least the story felt a bit tired. Somehow I never quite felt I connected with Dorian Christensen, latent leopard shapeshifter and member of Lucas's pack, or with Ashaya Aleine, Psy scientist whose conditioning is not only fracturing but who may have never been properly conditioned anyway. It doesn't seem like a great deal actually happens in this story and for those who haven't read the previous books the cast of characters is fairly large and might get confusing. I was less than convinced by Ashaya's sister Amara and the ways in which the two interacted (and I also found the similarity of the names confusing at times). Nalini Singh's worldbuilding continues in this novel with more about the council and another group, Pure Psy, which didn't always feel particularly connected to this story. I like the author's Psy/Changeling world but I found this book rather forgettable and nowhere near as good as 'Caressed by Ice'. It's a good read, worth the four stars, but not as good as some of the others.

It is time to wrap this series up! Before I even read this book I could have told you the following things were going to happen: * Someone would die. * Lulu would wear something "poison green" * Stephanie would be in a bind and call on the men in her life for help. * Something would blow up. * Stephanie would not be driving the same car at the beginning and end of the book. * Grandma Mauzer would go to at least one viewing at the funeral home. * Stephanie would sleep with both the men in her life. Well, I was right. This book is chick-lit-lite -- it took one evening to read and while enjoyable there was nothing that knocked my socks off. And it's getting hard to justify spending the money to buy the book instead of waiting to check it out of the library or borrowing it from a friend. In fact, I would recommend either of those options if you have not already purchased the book. It is time for Stephanie to make a decision and move on. Let this series end at 20 and call it a day. It's time for Stephanie Plum to grow up, her stupidity is getting tiresome. Let 19 be the book where she makes a choice and 20 be the wedding. It's time.

This Siker battery for my MacBook Pro honestly surprised me, it arrived looking brand new with just a single cycle count. I was a little skeptical at first, but performance has been rock solid and holds a charge way better than my original ever did. Swapping it in took maybe ten minutes. Totally worth it.

I was debating between the 10" or 14". Decided on this... Take it out the box, remove the cardboard, wipe off the factory grease, don't cut yourself, and you've now got the best hiking/camping knife made. Anything longer and you will lose the perfect balance and the thickness (strength). This straps perfectly to a molle web backpack with enough room to adjust, anything longer would be overkill.

Prior to buying these rings I was working out on a bar, and find the rings to be much more versatile and potent. They are easy to adjust for height (pullups to dips, for example), and setup was a snap - I have them hanging from an open joist in the garage. After a few weeks on these, my physique has popped, and strength is increased, I believe it's because I'm using more stabilizing muscles to perform a given exercise. There are many exercises that can be performed on the rings, even front and reverse flys, which are excruciating and maybe a bit much for me right now, but I never got this level of intensity from bar work. I also love that they rotate and follow my body's natural form, so there is much less stress on the joints and connective tissue, and I can feel the difference in my elbows, wrists, and shoulders. These are excellent quality and way more than worth the purchase price.
Not really effective at all. This is NOT tacky like the original product. It does not hold a candle in place at all. it is not thick.

This was my first book by Holly S. Roberts, and I can say, without hesitation, I'm a fan! Madison Abigail Kinlock aka Mak, is an ex-cop turned PI after a career ending injury. Now her days consist of investigating cheating douche bags, but as she likes to eat, she has no choice. Moon controls Arizona's largest crime syndicate. Mak and Moon cross paths when both are trying to catch the same guy, and set sparks flying everywhere. She can't get over how absolutely hot he is, and he's decided he wants her. After Mak finds herself in danger, she must turn to Moon for help, as she can't trust the police. Will Mak be able to keep the blue eyed Moon at arms length the more time they spend together? WOW! I LOVED LOVED LOVED this story so much! It started out with a bang, and I never caught my breath until I finished. The character development is great, with secondaries that I loved just as much. A fast paced, action packed, and very well written story line flows perfectly. Holly S. Roberts does a brilliant job in weaving layers of twists and turns that kept my heart racing, at warp speed, cover to cover. The well placed humor had me laughing until my sides hurt, and the sexy scene goodness is SCORCHIN' HOT! I'm very grateful that I had the opportunity to discover this talented author, and I CANNOT WAIT to read the next book in this Hotter than Hell series! HIGHLY HIGHLY HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

I've had this book on my Kindle for months now and I've already reread it three times, it holds up so well. The emotional layers don't fade, and I catch new little details each pass. Mackenzie and Theo's story sticks with you, like a worn-in hoodie that somehow gets more comfortable over time. Even knowing the twists, the writing still hits just as hard. Solid five stars, no question.

These are identical to pads I've been using under another name that cost 20% more from a retail store for 100 units. Excellent product for a small indoor dog. My guy can use the 24x24 size 3-4 times for pees before changing as there is no odor and plenty of room if he needs to do his little dance for #2 first thing in the morning.
Love how the 2-inch inseam keeps me cool on humid runs, but wish the waistband had a tiny zip pocket for keys.

I grabbed this after hearing the premise and really wanted to like it, but the execution just isn't there. The pacing feels off and the narrative keeps drifting into tangents that pull focus from what should've been a compelling story. It leans heavily on the author's personal backstory instead of building momentum. I stuck with it past the halfway point but eventually lost interest. Definitely not what I expected for the price.
It's decent but smaller than expected and the frame feels a bit flimsy when you move it around.
Got more than my money's worth here, tight pacing and a solid escape for under five bucks.
Got this thing months ago and it's still going strong. Used it on three rifles already, zero issues.

Fits beside the leather recliner and is perfect to hold the remotes, the coaster for the beer and on occasion, the phone. Inside there are the normal things a man considers essential for his survival, extra paper towels (for spills), sharpening stones for his pocket knife, extra batteries for the remotes, and other things unknown to this wife. Considering another for my chair. It is just about an inch below the top of the chair's arm. Makes it nearly perfect. The shelf below works great for holding the boots after they come off for the night. It went together easily, didn't have any trouble with the bottom shelf as others have reported. But then, this is not my first construction project. Was simple after making sure all the parts were there, reading the instructions (mostly pictures) and taking my time.
Very cute little dresses. My daughter is going to love these when she'll open them up on her birthday in one month. They are well stitched so I am impressed.

How can this tiny speaker sound so amazingly good? The crisp trebles and excellent bass response are hard to believe. This little thing is a bit shorter than two stacked Coke cans and about the same width. I now use this one speaker in my magic shows, although I think I'll move to two, although two are not necessary. BTW, I've used the speaker both outdoors and indoors with excellent results. I first heard the UEB2 at a show by a fellow magician, and I looked for his speaker and couldn't see it...until he showed me his little UEB2. Whaaaat? So I bought one the next day. Good idea.

My book arrived a day earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. It was packaged well, no bent corners or scuffed cover, just crisp and clean. Flipping through right away, the cover art looks even better in person and the print quality feels solid. I dove into the first chapter that same night and was immediately pulled into the intense back-and-forth between the leads. You can feel the tension simmering from page one. The only reason I'm holding back a star is that I spotted a handful of typos early on, which pulled me out of the moment a bit. Still, the story itself is addictive, and I'm excited to see where it goes.

I grabbed this as a cheap backup for paddleboarding and honestly didn't expect much, but it showed up two days early in really solid packaging, snug foam inserts, no rattling around. Unboxing felt way more premium than the price suggests. First thing I noticed was how light and grippy the body is, super easy to handle with wet hands. Popped in a card, took a few test shots around the yard, and the screen brightness surprised me. It's not instant between shots, but the colors looked crisp and natural right out of the box. For the money, I'm genuinely impressed. Can't wait to get it in the lake this weekend.
Exactly what I hoped for, lightweight, sturdy, and fits perfectly in my range bag.

GRRM is a god among writers. I grew up with a strong distaste for reading, instead preferring the easy entertainment of video games and TV. After discovering the Game of Thrones TV show on HBO i decided to thumb through one of the books. A few short months later i found i had ready every book in the series. If this book can make me like reading for fun i'm sure it will be an instant hot for anyone. The BEST book series ever written!

I wasn't sure what to expect from a book on Revelation, but this completely opened my eyes. Peterson doesn't get bogged down in end-times charts or scary symbolism, he helps you see the poetry and prayer woven through John's vision. It's less about decoding and more about letting the images shape your imagination and worship. I found myself slowing down, rereading passages, and actually enjoying a book of the Bible I used to avoid. His voice is warm and grounded, never academic or preachy. If you want something that feeds your spirit rather than just your curiosity, this is it. Grateful I picked it up.

For the price, this little drive is honestly a decent deal. I grabbed one because I wanted something super portable to keep on my keychain, and it definitely delivers on that, it's tiny, feels solid enough, and I haven't lost it yet. Where it falls short is speed, which I guess you'd expect at this price point. Moving a batch of photos or a movie file takes a while; I timed a 300MB folder and it was crawling at around 4 MB/s. It's fine for documents or occasional use, but if you're regularly shuttling bigger stuff, you'll get frustrated. Still, for ten bucks, I can't really complain too much. Just know what you're signing up for before you buy.

I wanted to love this since the color is spot-on, but the fit is wildly off. I'm usually a medium, ordered a large based on the chart, and it's still snug around the hips. Fabric is nice, just not cut for anyone with a shape.

Honestly, I grabbed these hoping for a cheap surround upgrade from my TV's built-in speakers, and they kinda land right in the middle. Setup was simple and they look fine under the TV, but the audio is pretty thin unless you crank the volume past 60%. The subwoofer helps a little, but it gets boomy instead of punchy on action scenes. Dialogue from the center channel sounds slightly muffled, so I had to boost treble way up just to make out voices clearly. For a small bedroom it's passable, but in a bigger living room you'll notice the lack of depth immediately. If you're expecting true surround immersion, this won't impress, but for casual use on a tight budget it does the bare minimum.

I was given this book as a Christmas gift, and I read it in a couple of hours. Albom presents a "heaven" without a God and without joy. I found it trite and depressing. As far as I can figure, Albom thinks heaven is a way to create closure and explanation for one's life that is not possible in this life. That makes far too little of both heaven and life on earth. It is NOT copper - its copper color (yellowish) and its plastic but it has a nice strong light.
I'd tell any fantasy lover to grab this, tight pacing, sharp dialogue, and characters that stick with you long after the last page.

I picked this up because I wanted the dirt on the early London club scene, and Keith doesn't disappoint. The way he describes those cramped, sweaty gigs where they were basically living on amps and borrowed cigarettes, man, you can almost smell the stale beer. What really grabbed me was his deep obsession with the blues records they were importing from Chicago. He doesn't just name-drop Muddy Waters; he breaks down specific riffs and why they hit him so hard as a teenager. You understand exactly how a bunch of scruffy art school dropouts turned that raw American sound into something completely their own. It's less a memoir and more a late-night jam session in book form.

I've re-read this three times now and it still hits every note. Weeks later, the scenes stick with me like glue. That gritty energy hasn't faded at all, it just settles deeper. My Kindle's been through a lot but this story holds strong.
Totally worth every penny, binged the whole thing in a weekend and already planning a rewatch.

After about three months, the Wi-Fi started dropping randomly, multiple times a day, and the only fix was a hard reboot. The plastic casing also feels super flimsy; one of the LAN ports cracked just from regular cable plugging and now it barely holds a connection. Over time, the signal strength degraded noticeably too, even in the next room. I've reset and reconfigured it more times than I can count, and it still overheats and locks up during heavy streaming. Honestly, for something that's supposed to last, it's fallen apart way too fast. Wouldn't recommend to anyone who values stability.

Unfortunately, the actual performance has been a constant headache. I'm on Windows 10 with a modern TP-Link router about forty feet away through one wall, and this thing drops the 5GHz band multiple times a day. Streaming anything in the evening is a buffering nightmare, and online gaming is basically unplayable with the random latency spikes. I've tried different USB ports, reinstalled drivers twice, and messed with router channels, nothing helps. It'll hold a solid link for an hour, then just fall off a cliff. The compact design is great, but reliability matters more, and this just doesn't deliver.

The plastic feels sturdy enough to survive plenty of drops on our hardwood floors, and the rounded edges give me peace of mind when he inevitably stumbles into it. Several of the science fiction "classics" I have read recently have proven to be rather less than classic for me. However, War of the Worlds is not one of them. I found it to be enjoyable, interesting, with an ability to keep me interested and engaged. I was surprised when I started the book that it is NOT like the Orson Wells version of radio and movie fame. This is set in England at the end of the 19th century. This definitely limits the defensive abilities of the Earthlings compared to the movie. It is also part of what makes it interesting, because the Martians react much differently than the movie version as well. They are slower, more definite in their movements, and more connected between landing spots. Also, there are only ten landing spots for the Martians, it seems, rather than the world-wide invasion of the movie version. In fact, one area that left me questioning was what the rest of the world was doing while this went on in southern England? The sample section of the book "The Map of the Sky" should have been left out, in my opinion. It was pompous and self-important, and was not in any way enjoyable, nor did it succeed in making me want to read a book that obviously was merely a "tag-along" story with little interest of its own. The writing style was excellent, fast-moving overall though not without some hefty explanation sections. Grammar, spelling, word use, all are what one would expect from a consummate professional. I held off reading this book for quite a while after I downloaded it, in the fear that it would be another disappointing "classic". It was nothing of the sort; it is an excellent book of early science fiction, and a very enjoyable read.
GIFT FOR HUSBAND, HAS SHARPENED EVERY KNIFE IN THE HOUSE. WORKS WELL, NO PROBLEMS

I went into Hard Eight with high expectations after loving the earlier books, and somehow Janet Evanovich still managed to surprise me. This one dials up the chaos in the best way, Stephanie Plum's luck is as disastrous as ever, and the situations she stumbles into had me laughing out loud on my commute. What really impressed me was how the humor balances with genuine suspense; there's a subplot involving her neighbor that actually had me nervous. The side characters shine too, especially Lula, who steals every scene she's in. Compared to other long-running series that lose steam, this entry feels fresh and tightly paced. It's the perfect mix of comfort and unpredictability, exactly what I want from a light mystery. Already ordered the next one.
I love having a sneaker that fits. I would have given them a 5 but one of the questions was does the shoe have good arch support and they have very little arch support.
