Every single action is spelled out: "I pick up the glass. I walk across the room." It got so repetitive that I started skimming paragraphs just to get back to the main plot. I did like the premise about a small-town bakery being a front for something darker, that had potential, but the execution killed any momentum. I kept putting it down and forgetting about it for days. By the end, I was just relieved it was over, which isn't how you want to feel finishing a book.
Still solid after weeks of my kid's rough play.
I thought it had great chemistry between the characters, I also thought it was really funny. I will read this book over and over. Has become one of my favorite alien love stories.
For under twenty bucks, I wasn't expecting much, but this thing is solid. My beagle pulls like crazy and it handles him with zero choking. Stitching's held up great after a month of daily use.
Classic. My grandbabies recite it with me as I read:-)

Slade and Samantha are getting married but will the secrets two people close to her between the happy ending she wants. Sam is back to being extra and running scared but she did grow a little bit in this book. Slade is trying his best to figure out who is trying to destroy his happiness but he is a fighter. We all know there is some secrets and two people close to her are hiding a big one. Can she forgive the ones trying to hurt get and the ones lying to her? Who knows you just have to read it to Sam and Slade get the happiness they been wishing for.
These keep my toddler busy for the whole bath, no more tears, just stacking fun.
Great cheese grater rasp. Sharp, lightweight, easy to clean out. Cuts wood fast, and the blade is replaceable. I should have gotten this sooner.
The pacing is relentless, I stayed up way too late because every chapter ends on a twist that dares you to stop.
Fits my road bike okay after a little fiddling, but the stock hardware just didn't cut it. Once set up, it's solid and does the job.

I've been using this flux mostly for reworking small circuit boards, and the no-clean residue is a lifesaver. It flows evenly without excessive splatter, even at lower temps. One syringe lasts forever since a tiny dab goes a long way on tight SMD pads. Really impressed with how easily it wets and cleans up.

Be aware that there are only three Melies shorts on this disk and only one of them is in color. A Trip to the Moon is on the disk twice, once in color and again in B&W. There is a choice of two excellent musical tracks on the B&W films, but the color film only has one soundtrack. It is completely unidiomatic, has distracting mumbling voices, and it doesn't follow the action of the film. Worse yet, it omits Melies's own narration that is supposed to be part of the film. Nothing makes sense without it. The cheezy synth music sounds like the loopy soundtrack to a 1970s mutton chop midget porn movie. The film is best watched with the volume turned down. The rest of the disk is filled out with a feature length documentary which is interesting, but I can't imagine watching it more than once. For a little more money you can get a five disk Flicker Alley DVD collection of Melies with dozens of great films, many of which are in even more beautiful color than this one. I'd recommend getting that instead. Search "melies flicker alley" and you'll find it. There is a supplement DVD that completes the set too. The DVDs actually look better than the color version here.

I picked this up on a whim and honestly got way more than my money's worth. For the price of a coffee, it's a solid little escape that doesn't skimp on personality. Lenore feels like someone you'd actually grab lunch with, she owns her curves, has a sharp tongue, and never once slips into that whiny damsel mode that cheapens so many shifter stories. The banter between her and the wolf is genuinely fun, and the pacing clips along without wasting a single page. No filler, no eye-rolling insta-love, just a tight, snappy read that left me grinning. If you're on the fence, just grab it, total bargain for an evening's entertainment.

I've read every Gamache book, so picking this up was a no-brainer, especially at the price. You get a lot of pages for your money, but the story itself felt sluggish and the usual charm was missing. Characters seemed to be going through the motions, and the mystery didn't hook me like I'd hoped. Still a decent value if you're a completist, just not a standout.
This bra does not offer the support I was looking for. Okay for smaller size cups
Perfect privacy filter for flights. I can work on sensitive stuff and the person next to me just sees a dark screen.

We use on the door that goes to the garage to keep hats and mittens in and on the door of our baby girls' room to keep her little matching outfits in. I like that the pockets are mesh in case items that are damp are place in there so that they can dry. I don't see much of a point to the double pocket. The hangers that came with it are useless as they do not fit over ANY door that is in our house. I ended up spending about as much money on 3M command hooks to hang it as I did on the organizer itself, but it works.

This litter used to be a solid pick when it was more affordable. Clumps decently and keeps odor down for a day or two, but at the current price point it's just not worth it for multi-cat homes. It's still heavy to scoop and lug to the trash. I'm mixing it with cheaper alternatives now to stretch it, which helps but isn't ideal. Three stars because it works fine, just overpriced.

I like the shoe but I should have gone 1 1/2 size smaller. I normally wear size 12 in just about every other shoe I own except for Chuck Taylors. These fit ok with regular socks but I usually wear these with thin or no socks. Bottom Line I am a 12 in all other shoes (New Balance Minimus with no socks) and I should have gone with 10 1/2. I actually now have 2 other CT's, the leather city hikers and a leather low, both 10.5 and both fit perfectly.
I love the color selection! Could use a green one though.
The fabric is surprisingly soft and breathable, I wore it hiking in chilly weather and it kept my neck perfectly warm without overheating.
I loved this show years ago when I watched it with my son. I just enjoyed watching it with my grandsons!!
These are really cute, a bit small but since you can adjust the string it's ok. It comes with extra pom poms which is really nice
Thought this series would be good, but it honestly blew past every expectation.
Got a great deal on this and honestly, it looks way more expensive than it was. Super cozy fabric and holds up well after a wash. Really happy for the price.
Great cup. Will last forever. Keeps things cool / warm.

I wasn't expecting much for the price, but this little card completely surprised me. Setup on my older Canon took maybe two minutes, just popped it in, entered the code on my phone, and photos started appearing in my gallery almost instantly. No cables, no pulling the card out after every shoot, which alone saves me so much hassle. Image transfers are quick enough for my needs, and I haven't noticed any battery drain issues some folks mention. For what it costs compared to upgrading to a newer WiFi-enabled body, it's an absolute steal. It basically breathed new life into my trusty DSLR. If you're on the fence, just grab one. Best bang-for-the-buck camera accessory I've bought in ages.
My toddler spends ages twisting, shaking, and chasing this thing around the living room. Keeps those busy little hands happy.
Love this but its very flimsy legs and the remote worked for 1 day:(
Loved loved my book. goot it fast. I'm not a big reader, unless it's hot and steamy. I read it in 3 days. I can't wait till the 3rd book comes out!!
Got so much useful info for under five bucks, feels like a steal.
The good acting was a given for me and I was not disappointed. The plot, the mood and the plot twist is what makes me appreciate this film.
Decent for guest beds but not firm enough for my back.

Box arrived earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was solid, thick cardboard, molded foam inserts, and every piece individually wrapped. No scuffs or dents on any of the panels, so first impressions were really positive. Hardware was packed in a clearly labeled blister pack, and the manual was actually readable, which feels rare these days. I laid everything out and checked against the parts list, and nothing was missing. The finish looks clean and consistent up close, no weird smells or sharp edges. I haven't tackled assembly yet, but based on what I've seen so far, it seems well-made and thoughtfully packed. Giving it four stars for now, with that last star depending on how smoothly the build goes.
My dog's favorite toy. (He's a Maltese, 8 pounds) He will retrieve it,or play tug of war with it, and comes running when he hears the squeaker.

At 100 pages long, "Cowboy Crazy" relied on some trope (including a corny one) to move the plot along, but it was an enjoyable one. Hank Dalton received an ultimatum... Get married to inherit part of the family ranch... Or get left with nothing. Hank, the oldest, loved to run the ranch, but living two hours from civilization limits his choice of women. When his four brothers left for the city to pick up some girls, he chose to stay home... and got to rescue a stranded motorist... Charlotte Halton. Charlie was almost killed when her crazy BF drugged her and burned her apartment down, leaving her scarred both inside and out. She decided for a fresh start... and getting stranded in Paradise Valley in the middle of cattle country wasn't part of her plan... but it just may be Hank's luck. Hank knew it was fate that delivered a woman to him, but she's skittish. Can Hank help her find a new start? Or will she run away again? I personally don't like "run away to sort my feeling" endings, but there's a grand gesture to mitigate that, so at least that's good.

I've had this ramp for about four months now, and it's held up okay structurally but the surface is already wearing down in spots. The black gritty layer started peeling near the center after maybe six weeks of my 70-pound golden retriever using it daily. I ended up stapling an old yoga mat over it, which actually gives better grip anyway. The hinges still feel solid and it folds up fine, but I've noticed a slight wobble when it's fully extended on concrete. No major cracks or breaks yet, just feels a little less sturdy than when it was new. For light occasional use it's probably fine, but if you're using it multiple times a day expect to make some tweaks.

For the price, I expected a story that actually goes somewhere. Instead, you get a tangled mess of perspectives, John, Rylynn, Kyle, Dex, Rae, and half a dozen others, none of which feel fully developed. Every subplot fizzles out before anything meaningful happens. It's like paying for a full meal and getting a sampler platter where everything tastes unfinished. Not worth it.

My boy cannot get enough of this. I`ve gone thru both of them already. () This toy is actually very durable, he just play with it that \_()_/ much. I live in a two story house and my kitty will drag it all the way upstairs to play. He runs and jumps until he's panting. Great exercise for my cat, and fun for me, too! I received this as a gift to share my experiences while using this product.

The mic monitoring is fine for calls, but I bought this hoping it would clean up my audio for streaming. The passthrough introduces a faint static hiss that my interface doesn't pick up otherwise. It's subtle, but once you notice it, it's hard to ignore. Build is solid and the controls are straightforward, which I appreciate. For basic phone or office use it's probably fine, just didn't meet my slightly higher expectations at this price.

Nice fit, but mine wore out in less than 2 months of use! I use these just over 2x per week. Already, the glove material is separating and flaking off from the hand pads. See photos. Now, I had a pair of bicycle gloves that lasted me over 1 year doing the same workout as these for my weight training regime. When my bike gloves finally kicked the bucket, I thought, I guess I should have purchased weight training gloves to use for weight training. My next set was Champion C9 weight training gloves, which separated at the seem after about 4 months. I attributed that to the low cost/low quality and decided to upgrade to the more expensive GoFit gloves. Nope, these things came apart in less than 2 months of use, worst return yet...and most expensive!! Go figure.
The leather feels premium and the stitching is solid, but they took a few wears to break in comfortably.

I've had these clippers for about eight months now, and they're still going strong after grooming my hyper little schnauzer mix every few weeks. The blades have held their edge way better than I expected, no tugging or snagging even through his thick, wiry coat. I did oil them after every third use, which probably helped. The motor hasn't bogged down once, and the housing has survived a couple of drops onto tile without a crack. Honestly, for the price I figured they'd be disposable, but they're solid little workhorses. My dog still hates grooming day, but at least the tool holds up to his squirming.

My 2-year-old asks for this Barney every morning and it instantly puts her in a happy mood. The songs are clear, volume is just right, and the plush is super soft for hugs. She drags it everywhere, car rides, grocery runs, bedtime. Battery life has been solid through heavy daily use. Honestly, seeing her dance and sing along makes it worth every penny. Great quality toy.

I have mixed emotions about this water filter system. I love how it is hung from a tree, and doesn't require pumping, but I did run into problems. I used it for a week in Boundary Waters, at least the bag. After 4 days,providing water for 6 people, the filter was completely clogged, and is incapable of being back flushed. The pre-filter screen was not fine enough to catch the "material/ suspected tannin" in the BWCA water. I eventually attached a Sawyer inline filter to the outlet hose which got me through the last 4 days. My Base Camp Pro did come with the new and improved filer, but it couldn't handle what I needed it to. I love the idea, but was pretty disappointed.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up completely hooked by the vivid stories about lesser-known figures. The way it ties geology to human ambition makes every chapter feel like uncovering a hidden layer of America.

I've gone through so many USB-C cables that either quit after a month or just refused to fast-charge my tablet. This Anker one has been rock solid for weeks now. The 6-foot length gives me plenty of reach, and it handles my phone, earbuds, and power bank without any hiccups. Super happy with this purchase.

This book held up beautifully through multiple reads, which says a lot since I tend to crack spines and dog-ear pages. The paperback binding stayed tight and the cover barely showed wear after tossing it in my bag for weeks. Story-wise, the emotional push and pull kept me hooked even when I revisited certain chapters. I did feel the middle dragged a tiny bit, but the payoff made up for it. Solid construction and a plot that lingers.

Pretty good product. It's way better made than the Luggable Loo toilet seat. It's a thicker plastic and there is also a rubber seal inside the lid to give it a snug fit when you close it. The rubber seal does in fact help suppress and odors. This seat DOES fit the orange Home Depot 5 gallon bucket and both white 5 gallon Walmart buckets. You do have to apply a good amount of pressure the first time. I think it's a good value purchase.
Great value and well made! They added a lot to my parents' anniversary party and we plan on reusing them for a birthday part next month.
My sister just bought one for her salon after seeing mine, it's a total game-changer for ugly walls.

I cannot say enough to warn you away from this pillow - please save your money. My husband bought this pillow hoping it would be as stellar as its description and it falls short in every way. After 2 months of trying to make it work (because it was SO expensive), he gave up and returned to his old pillow. I then took up the cause (because it was SO expensive) and tried to make it work. - There is not a slant from either side that is comfortable for your neck - The material collapses with the weight of your head - No benefit to snoring - Not comfortable if you sleep on your back (my husband) - Not comfortable if you sleep on your side (me) So after months of trying to literally make this work, I emailed about getting a return because MOST other expensive pillow companies have 1 or 5 year guarantees....not this place. They give you so many days to return it. My mistake to trust people because they are nurses. Why would nurses sell pillows? Now we have this pillow no one likes, even our house guests have tried it and preferred other, older pillows to this one!
For what I paid, these held up way longer than I expected with my heavy chewer.

I got this as a Christmas gift for my wife after one of the grandkids broke the screen on her last one. Not very pleased with the quality of the cameras or the screen resolution but it does what she wants it to do. The colors are great it's just a little grainy if your looking at pictures or watching a video and the pictures from the camera are very grainy. This was a disappointment to her because she likes to take it when we go hiking and use it for pictures. This one won't work well for that due to poor camera quality. I wanted to get the same one she had but couldn't find it on amazon. Maybe they don't sell it anymore. The tagital seems to respond fast enough for browsing the net and playing her games. The touch screen is sensitive enough. We only had one issue after a few hours. It worked great with the included keyboard at first then last night it wouldn't boot up, kept getting an error message. Then we unplugged the keyboard and it booted correctly. Haven't tried the keyboard again to see if it was a fluke or the keyboard actually went bad. The included case is very nice and I like the way the tablet actually is snapped in place. Much more secure than the one she had with her other tablet. For the price I paid we are not overly disappointed. In short if you want to just browse or play games buy it. If you want to take pictures and watch videos I'd look else where. This is the first thing I've purchase through Amazon that I wasn't 100% happy with.

I really was disappointed when I received this. It had white/gray spots on the inside. Not sure what it was, but it looked a little moldy. I used professional mold remover and a mold shield ( Zep Brand ) and treated it heavy for 2 days to make sure whatever it was is gone, and won't come back. This fits perfectly in my Chevy Avalanche's Saddle bag, and hold's all my ropes, tie-downs and bungee's great!

I'm a weekend gravel rider and this little pump has been a lifesaver. It tucks away neatly under my top tube and doesn't interfere with my frame bag at all. Even with a smaller barrel, getting up to road pressures doesn't take forever, and the switch to high-volume mode makes seating tubeless tires way less of a headache. The included bracket fits standard mounts perfectly, no zip ties or Velcro needed. Honestly, I'd suggest it to any commuter, bikepacker, or casual roadie who wants something unobtrusive but capable. It's so affordable that I picked up a second one for my partner's hybrid.

The first Pilots Hockey novel featured Aleksandr, a Russian Hockey player who claimed to need a translater, and Auden, an American who knows Russian because she was raised by her grandfather. This is a cute story, but part of it didn't really mesh well for me. Aleksandr first off didn't really need someone to translate. I can get past that part but there's a lot of talk about all the guys who want to be with Auden throughout and it gets a little ridiculous. Also, the breakup, which inevitably happens in romance novels, was over dramatic and for a ridiculous reason. But all in all it was a good story and I did want to read the next one when I finished it.

Perfect! It's exactly what I wanted and he loves it. My husband is a very large muscular man, 6 ft 265 lbs and this necklace fits him great; even with a thick neck and large traps! Not too delicate to snap and not too large to be gansta'! Perfect!

I loved this story from start to finish. Alana Ankh has created a whole new world that is filled with magic and is very compelling. When Ivy and Kris meet they are not supposed to have a connection to each other because they come from different races and have a different set of cultural norms however despite these superficial differences they can't stop thinking about each other. When Kris devises a way to get Ivy with him again it works and Ivy becomes Kris's concubine; flouting the wishes of his parents and breaking a betrothal. Ivy and Kris have a kind of whirlwind romance that has political repercussions for Kris. This story has love at first sight, fights both physical and verbal, societal pressure, and two men that fight through all of that nonsense to make a real life together.
these camera batteries are not from Canon but they hold a charge better and they also last longer than the original Canon batteries

Order arrived two days early and was packed super securely, no scuffs or scratches anywhere. Unboxing felt premium, everything looked sharp and well-made. Tried it in the pool immediately and it fit perfectly. Seriously impressed right out of the gate.
I had sharpening stones for years but do to moving several times, I've lost them. I love this sharpening system because it has three grades of stone in one small package.

I wasn't sure what to expect for the price, but this little puppet is fantastic. The stitching is neat, the colors are bright, and my daughter hasn't put it down since we opened the box. It's soft, easy for her to maneuver, and feels well-made. Honestly, you'd think it cost twice as much. Perfect for imaginative play without breaking the bank.

I wanted to love this case, it's slim, the magnets wake the tablet reliably, and the color looked sharp right out of the box. But after about two months of mostly indoor use, the edges started fraying and the outer layer is bubbling near the spine where it bends. I'm not rough on my gear; this just rides in my bag for work and sits on the nightstand. For the price, I expected it to hold up longer before looking shabby. It still functions fine as a stand and cover, so it's not a total loss, but the durability is a letdown. Decent short-term, disappointing long-term.
I wasn't a fan of the Star Trek franchise before I saw this movie but I actually really loved it and am super excited for the second one.
Took a chance on this and now our whole kitchen smells fresh again, finally.

Why do I love this show? I want every character on this show to be my best friend (except Robin). They're funny, caring, and awesome. The actors truly have tons of chemistry, providing a quality show in a half-hour block. This is no generic sitcom. Sure, it's got a laugh track and some of the banter is cliché, however, most of it is incredibly witty and heartfelt. Ted is engaged in a genuine search for love, and I'm completely wrapped up in it. I fell in love with the Cake Girl (Victoria, played by Ashley Williams) and was sad to see her go (it's probably why I'm not a huge fan of Robin). This show has continuity and a compelling storyline; it simply amazes me. It's very quotable. I find myself quoting with friends I've talked into watching it (who get equally hooked). The cast is brilliant, as well as the director and writers. Bloc Party's song: `This Modern Love' at the close of Season One clenched it for me though, dramatically building up a powerful finale I won't soon forget. It joins the hall of fame for greatest season finale songs with the likes of The O.C's: Imogen Heap `Hide and Seek' and Grey's Anatomy's: Snow Patrol `Chasing Cars'. Hands down, this is the best sitcom I've ever seen. Buy it, watch it, love it. DISCLAIMER: Though the writers are good at what they do, don't listen to any of their commentaries. They are exceptionally boring, lackluster, and have slightly soured my enjoyment of the show. It's that bad.

I've gone through a couple of bulky travel cases that always felt awkward to lug through the airport, but this setup is a total game changer. The support rod slides in easily and gives me real peace of mind that my clubs won't get crushed. It's incredibly light, fits my soft cover perfectly, and everything arrived in perfect shape after a cross-country flight. Highly recommend for any traveling golfer.

Got this expecting full wireless master control, but it's pretty limited there on my 6D. Still, exposure is consistent and high-speed sync is smooth with my 430EX. Does the core stuff really well for the price. Solid, just not quite the all-in-one solution I hoped for.

Grew up loving this movie and finally grabbed the Blu Ray. The restoration is gorgeous, colors pop and the sound is crisp. My kids are now obsessed with the drawing tutorial in the extras, which was a neat surprise. No DVD combo pack, but for the price I paid, I'm not complaining. We've watched it three times this week already. A total family favorite that holds up beautifully.
My four year old grandson just loves this puzzle. He puts it together every time he comes to our house!

This watch is a real beauty, the red dial really sets it off. I like that it is very inexpensive, so I wear it daily with no worries. Be aware that it does not have a date function, nor does the bezel rotate. The chronograph is fully functional. The watch comes in a large plastic waterproof container which is really interesting. The case is odd, but I like it. The band length is enormous, I have a 9 inch wrist and still took out four links, so if you have a large wrist you are in luck. Don't forget to get a link remover when you order this, one is not included. Tip: One side of the pins look like screw heads...you want to tap the pins out from the other side. There are arrows on the back of the links that also indicate the correct direction for pin removal.
Let me tell you - when I say I was on the edge of my seat the entire book I mean that! These characters are CRAZY & book 2 is about to be lit. Smh. I just know its going down

Arrived a day early and the packaging was flawless, no bent corners or scuffs, which I always appreciate. I cracked it open the second it landed on my doorstep and honestly, within the first few pages I was already grinning. The tone just clicks right away. Super happy with the condition and how quickly I got sucked in.
cups came chipped and missing blue coating. I ordered 6 and two were chipped. The others are very poorly made.

His favorite movie, done up in a beautiful Voight-Kampff case... he should write this review because I will get all the good things wrong. He loves it to death and can't stop going over the pieces, even years after I bought it for him.

Update: What a difference a few hours can make! I originally rated this blender at 3 stars earlier today and have now changed my rating to 1 star after using the blender a second time - after using it extremely lightly twice it cracked near the blade (see the product picture I've uploaded). I was blending liquids so I can't imagine this thing surviving crushing things as the description states. If you're smart you'll avoid this blender and pay a few more dollars for one that's more durable. Original review: This is a nice basic hand/stick blender. The price was right for what I needed it for - purchased for soap making so I didn't want to spend a ton of money on it. That being said, I'll probably look for a different one the next time around because this one ended up being bigger and more awkward than I was expecting and I have large hands (if I had smaller hands I definitely wouldn't like using it). The end with the blade detaches for cleaning which is nice but the blade assembly itself doesn't. Pros: Price Cons: The whole thing is bigger than I expected and it's awkward to hold and use (you'd have a hard time using it in a glass to blend something small) The blade doesn't come off for cleaning Not durable
a good story. a little too rushed, same elements, same good plot, but not enough transitioning as in earlier books. ending made me go???
My two month old chihuahua puppy really likes the blue and the gray bone, the other one has no appeal to her, no smell or roughness... it's worth it for the first two bones!
If you are reading this review you probably already own a pair of Chucks. I love the look and feel of the Lo Top ones. They look great and feel great.

I ordered this product based on other positive reviews that I read and I am happy to say that it works great. You get what you pay for. Even the bulb setting works perfectly. I recommend this wireless shutter for anyone who wants a great accessory for their camera but can't see paying Canon outrageously overpriced fee.

Bought the 24-inch MidWest iCrate for my rescue Chihuahua mix after struggling with a flimsy wire crate that rattled every time she shifted. Honestly expected decent quality given the brand reputation, but it blew past that. The assembly took maybe five minutes with zero tools, and every connection locked in solid. No sharp edges, no wobble. The divider panel was a lifesaver during potty training, adjusted it twice as she grew. The plastic pan slides out smoothly and doesn't warp when I hose it down. My pup settled in faster than any dog I've crate trained before. Feels sturdy enough to last through future puppies. Seriously impressive for the price point.
I love these shoes. They are very comfortable and perfect for running.
Got this for my 11 year old granddaughter and she loved it.

Arrived in perfect condition with really thoughtful packaging, no scuffs or dings. Pulled it out and it immediately felt substantial and smooth. The wood grain is gorgeous in person, way nicer than the listing photos. Super happy with this purchase.
I expected a basic plush but the fur is incredibly soft and the stitching is top-notch. It honestly looks way better in person than the listing photos.
Way too pricey for what's basically a bag of dried cardboard. My pup sniffed once and walked off.

I've been following this series for years, and this installment focusing on Levet is an absolute gem. The pacing kept me turning pages late into the night, and the humor mixed with genuine heart made the whole experience so satisfying. It's rare to see a side character step into the spotlight so seamlessly, and the author nailed his voice. The story balances action and tender moments perfectly, and I found myself grinning like an idiot during several scenes. If you've been rooting for Levet through all the chaos of the earlier books, this one feels like a well-earned payoff. Just a fun, feel-good read that left me eager for whatever comes next. Highly recommended.
I tore through this in two sittings, warm, unexpected, and exactly the kind of story that leaves you smiling long after the last page.

Great training tool for my little puppy. She learned to walk really well with this. Only drawback is that it chafed her armpit if she pulled more. Also, would be better if the clasps were up higher rather than at the armpit. This also is what chafed her.

Very Fine Mesh Strainers by Zenpro, (Set of 3) Stainless Steel - Strain Pasta Noodles Quinoa, Tea, Sift & Sieve Flour Well made (made from stainless steel), strong and for me has multiple uses.great value for money and comes with a set of measuring spoons which is also a big plus and make this a great value for money. The strainers come in 3 sizes that nest into each other making them easier to store. Easy to clean and dishwasher safe these make a great addition to any kitchen from the occasional cook to the professional chef, this would also make a great house warming or home gift for a cook. They also come with a lifetime warranty so you really can not go wrong! I received this product at a discount price for my honest review
Sounds great when you turn all the effects off. Can be a pain to get the drivers going. The included mic is a joke.

I wanted to love this little bag for daily commute use, but the zipper situation makes it hard to recommend without a warning. They catch constantly, especially around the corners, and I've had to baby them from day one just to avoid a full split. The size and low-key look are perfect if you only carry a tablet, a slim notebook, and a few pens, honestly, I'd say it's fine for someone who barely opens their pack during the day. If you're in and out of your bag often, the hassle adds up fast.

We have been using this for about a month, and are really satisfied. I was looking for a solid navy rug for my sons room and the price could not be beat. The navy isn't too dark, so it does look like a true navy and matches nicely with other decor. Im not sure Id put this in a high traffic area, but it works well for a bedroom. It had a new carpet smell for a few days, but it quickly went away.
Sturdy and well made, and my cockatiel seems to be quite pleased with it. The little clippy bit allows me to swap out the toy often to keep her interested.

This third book kept me turning pages late into the night, and the chemistry between the leads really worked. Some plot twists felt a tiny bit predictable, but the cozy small-town vibe pulled me right in. Definitely a solid, entertaining read overall.
For this price, the chemistry between them is totally worth it.

Vetri-Science Dental Stix are not a dog treat, they are a supplement designed to help "periodontal health" in dogs, and should not be used as a treat. Their purpose is to prevent plaque formation and clean teeth. This explains the higher price. I have previously tried C.E.T. Oral Hygiene Chews with my dog, and although they seemed to work well, the smell was horrible. These Dental Stix do not have a bad smell, and are in almost the same shape as Greenies. I have been giving my dog one a day for the last month, and although it's difficult to determine how effective they actually are since they're designed to prevent plaque formation, I can say my dog has great-looking teeth, and his breath seems to have improved some. My vet, who I have a lot of trust in, says that these products aren't "junk science" and do seem to work fairly well, although he didn't have a preference over brand. Since I've seen the results of bad dental hygiene on 5+ year old dogs, I will likely continue to use these although not every day) to promote good oral health for my dog.

Dell laptops are not as evil as the 3 lower comments make them seem. It takes some basic user knowledge to get accustomed with the product. There is no need to 'knock' Dell down because of a bad user experience. Blue screens are usually caused by bad programming or writing of software. Blue screens have codes that say "error: xxx" where XXX is the name and code of the problem. MOST bluescreens are MEMORY PARITY problems -- bad programming of software. Dell not responding to customer issues is absurd. I have never had that experience in the 15 years I've been contacting Dell for any situation. My suggestion to people who have "so much trouble" is to figure out why others do not. You may not realize that installing and uninstalling through the Windows installer is the proper way to install. Installing through each individual programs installer is WRONG. This advice will fix 9% of problems out there dealing with "my laptop is slow now!"

The Fulcrum 30030-308 wireless remote LED light is a simple and effective way to add light to a room or a closet that doesn't already have a light and a light switch. It's a battery-operated setup and remarkably easy to install with double-backed tape. In fact, there is an on/off switch on the light itself so you don't even need to use the wireless remote to control the light if you're just adding the light to a small hallway closet or under a cabinet to light up a workbench. The built-in adjustable timer works great and automatically turns off the light after a pre-selected period of time (to save battery life) if you forget to turn off the light. My only minor complaint is about the size of this set. I'm glad the light is "tall" from top to bottom and "wide" from side to side so that the light source is large enough to be both bright and soft instead of looking like a flashlight... but the light fixture itself is pretty "thick" or "fat" from front to back and that makes it less than ideal if you're trying to hide this light under a cabinet or on the wall in a small closet. Similarly, the remote light switch is unusually large compared to how small other wireless on/off remotes are. Bottom line, this works and I'm mostly happy with it, but I'd be even happier if the light fixture was "flatter" from front to back and the remote light switch was smaller.

The pad is exceptional for traction or laser. I currently have two testable units.. a 15"+ PC and 8"+ netbook. It's great for both, but the larger proves that this desk is the perfect fit as it is invisible once placed. Usage appeal for me is, ironically, in bed. I have bought several options for bed use in the past, but always found them to be cumbersome or difficult to move etc. And god forbid you had to get up quickly. With this desk, you simply move your computer instead of having to move one then the other. Then there is car use. No more lugging something to avoid burning your legs. A simple take-along and it slides right under the seat. I have not used it for travel or airplane, but I see this as a great option if needed. My PC is getting "older" and I feel it gets hotter than it should at times, but this is a great remedy. The hollow core keeps it cooler and safer. This doorbell system has a LOT of potential. What I've found, though, is that it does NOT seem to like cold weather. I went through two sets of batteries in two months. I learned and bought a large set of batteries through Amazon to accommodate the cold weather months (I live at 5,000 feet elevation). Customer service is EXTREMELY prompt and courteous. After my first doorbell transmitter blew through a set of batteries in under a month, they replaced the unit. The second unit's battery door doesn't shut all the way and I had to tape it shut. In 'normal' temperatures, it seems to be fine. But when the battery starts to go bad, the doorbell will go off on its own randomly, many times in a short span of time. There is no other way to tell if the battery is dying, other than that or missing guests:( I'm going to keep using this system, though, knowing what I know about it. The price is unbeatable, you get what you pay for, and I'm sincerely hopeful that they can improve the design for future models, because the chimes are fine (I use the default 'ding-dong, ding-dong' one) and I'm alerted to guests in a building out behind my home where I cannot otherwise monitor the front door. NOTE: because this doesn't exactly look like a doorbell to guests, I applied a printed label that I made with my label printer (black text on yellow tape) that says "DOORBELL" on it and applied it to the surface of the transmitter to alert people to the bell. Guests verified that my label alerted them to the existence of a doorbell as they would otherwise not have known I had one because it blends in to the white paint on my house.

I tried taking pictures in a theatre and had to wait literally about 30 seconds in between shots... which is terrible when you're trying to capture something as it's happening! When I loaned the camera to someone, she told me, "I'd return it if I were you. I have no idea how you stand that." Thing is, I love just about everything else about it. If I had the money, I'd buy a different camera to use indoors and keep this one for the outdoors/good lighting shots. The other negative is that it really isn't great with red eyes. My kitchen-sink faucet has a one-point attachment Moen 7594SRS Arbor One-Handle High Arc Pull Down Kitchen Faucet, Spot Resist Stainless. I guess that a good deal of torque is applied to the single nut and bolt holding it in place, and it loosens easily, at least when installed without Locktite as my plumber did. It's almost impossible to get at this nut. I could feel it in order to hand-tighten it blindly, but I couldn't get it secured. I thought getting a wrench on it without a special plumber's faucet tool would be impossible. I was therefore nearly ready to give up and request a service call, that surely would have cost $100, when I saw this product. It came in 2 days, and my faucet was secured rigidly (pun intended, sorry) in 2.01 days -- a minor miracle from my desperate perspective. I wish it had multiple inserts for different size nuts because if they're mounted flush, I don't see how it could reach the smaller sizes, as designed with the smaller-sized wrenches nested within and below the largest. That didn't matter in my case, but it would limit the usefulness somewhat under some circumstances. Other reviews would lead one to believe that it's too flimsy to be useful, and that's certainly UNtrue. The construction is plenty strong enough for hand-tightening or loosening, which is all it's designed for and which should almost always be sufficient. I can see how you might crack or break it if you tried to turn the lower end with a pipe wrench. The slot, which is the genius-inspired part of this tool, could allow it to deform under that kind of torque, but it's not designed for that use. Highly recommended; an inexpensive solution to potentially expensive home-maintenance problems.

Why tinker with a masterpiece? This original movie version of the stage play was remade twice for the screen and once for television and all of them are not just poor remakes, they are AWEFUL in comparison. The 1950's musical remake, "The Opposite Sex" fails in many ways. First, by turning it into a musical. Second, by casting June Allison in the lead. And third, by adding men to the cast. The latest remake from a few years ago fails by trying to modernize the storyline. The story works best when set in the 1930's pre-"women's lib". This 1939 version is much more biting and satiracal, the actresses give their all for director George Cukor. As superb as Joan Crawford, Norma Shearer and Rosiland Russell are, for me, the stand-out performances were by the three older actresses, Mary Boland, Marjorie Main, and especially Lucile Watson.

I bought this mainly as an emergency television for power outages. Works great. The batteries that came with it are awful, two different unique batteries, expensive to replace and one went bad fairly quickly (it would not hold a charge and it swelled up, popping the cover off of the battery compartment). I bought the docking station and use Eneloop rechargeable batteries in it. Out of the package, the rechargeables ran the TV for 2 hours and 15 minutes. The batteries tend to last longer after they have been charged the first time.

Love these. These shorts are so lightweight, you'll almost forget you're wearing them. Perfect for kickboxing or grappling. Highly recommended checking them out for all your mma needs. Look great with the sanbul rashguard as well;-)

Too flimsy for a large iguana. It was like waling a St. Bernard with dental floss! I am on a roll right now. I have to read every book back to back. They are so good, they are hard to resist. Short and spicy and Mae is such a match maker.

I grabbed this for self-study and honestly expected to be in over my head, but for the price it's an absolute steal. Yeah, it's dense and assumes you're comfortable with second-year math, but the way concepts are laid out makes the climb feel manageable. I've paid triple for technical books that explain half as well. The problem sets are tough but fair, and working through them actually builds real intuition instead of just pattern matching. If you're willing to put in the hours, this book delivers a proper engineering-level understanding without needing a Stanford tuition bill. Easily one of the best value purchases on my shelf.
Seam popped within second use. Since then the seam has popped in several other places. We still use it, but I don't know how much longer it will stay in one piece.
Captivating storyline with scenes painted so realistically they were vivid with believable life. Wonderful descriptions and insight into ancient times.

I'm honestly surprised by how much this spruced up my bedroom. The fabric feels substantial without being stiff, and the pattern adds just the right amount of texture. It's held up great through a couple washes already, no fading or loose threads. Definitely a small upgrade that made my whole bed feel more pulled together.

Even for zero dollars, this wasn't worth the time. The dialogue loops endlessly and the language feels forced for shock value. I wanted a believable love story, not a string of cheap cliches. Save your reading time, there's no real payoff here.

Honestly, it's decent if you're just lounging on the couch or napping upright in a chair at home. For actual travel, though, it slides around too much. I'd only recommend it to someone who stays mostly still while sleeping, tossers and turners will probably get frustrated.

I picked this up hoping to simplify my living room setup, and it absolutely delivered. Paired it with a 55-inch Samsung and had everything running in under ten minutes. The wireless connection to the subwoofer was rock solid right out of the gate, no fiddling with settings or re-syncing. Dialogue comes through crisp and clear, and there's enough low-end punch to make action movies feel immersive without rattling the walls. Streaming music over Bluetooth is seamless too, and the sound stays balanced even at higher volumes. For a compact system at this price point, the overall experience has been way better than expected. It just works, and it sounds great doing it.
Smaller than expected but are very well made! Smell like they were made and shipped right off. Bought these for my 2 year old daughter. Like the cute story behind the boxes that comes with it.

I snagged this cleaner on a whim because I didn't want to spend a fortune replacing the runner in my hallway that my dog and two kids had absolutely destroyed over the winter. For the price, I figured even if it just freshened it up a bit I'd be happy. I was genuinely shocked when I pulled it out of the wash, the muddy paw prints and a mystery grape juice stain near the fringe were completely gone. It dried soft, didn't shrink, and the colors actually looked brighter. Saved me easily a hundred bucks versus buying a new rug. If you've got tired rugs you're ready to toss, give this a shot first.

I was genuinely impressed by the crisp, clean transfers on this set. I'd only ever caught grainy syndicated reruns late at night, so seeing the first season with this level of detail was a real treat. The moody black-and-white photography pops in a way I didn't expect, and it totally elevates the eerie atmosphere of each episode. John Newland's understated hosting style comes through perfectly, and the subtle sound design, those little audio cues that ratchet up the tension, is crystal clear. My favorite part is the restoration of the original music cues; they add so much to the psychological creepiness. If you're a fan of classic anthology television, this is an easy recommendation. It feels like discovering the show all over again.

I bought 4 bibs...I had an issue with one of them, the zipper failed...I contacted the seller, they responded immediately!! and offered to replace the item asap! no hassle. I am extremely pleased with them and the warmth of these bibs...very comfortable too! I will consider purchasing a few more as gifts.

after geting mine the unit fell off the ring it was atached to as a little swing pressure on the unit caused the 2 halfs to spread thus leting the atached ring fall out. the first time it did it on my driveway and i did not find it till after I ran over it about 4 times I fixed it by using some super glue to make sure the two halfs no longer spread and it working fine to this day but I also suffer from the week latching when it is extended but that a dealable issue for me as all you have to do is keep for finger on the button. update apr 2012 7 years later works just like new after seven years in fact the only problem is it gotten so small in size as 512 does not cut it anymore

Got two of these for my almost eight year old for Christmas and everyone in the family loves them, including my husband and I! It does take some practice to learn how to fly them. Practice learning how to go up and down and hovering before you try to move left, right, forward or backward. Also very pleased with how resilient they are! Each of us crashed them on our first attempts to fly them, including a couple of crashes on our high ceilings and all the back down to the ground, and they still run great! They run for about 20 minutes of flight time and take about 40 minutes to charge. Based on reviews I read if other flying toys or drones that is a pretty good ratio. You won't be disappointed! I highly recommend these helicopters!!
This lamp feels sturdy and looks great on my desk. The light is soft on the eyes, and setup took maybe two minutes. Really happy with it.
Arrived fast, packed solid, and looks really well built.

Good enough for a backup tool kit in the truck or shed. The action is a little stiff, but it bites fine on stuck bolts. I'd recommend it to DIYers who only need occasional use and don't want to overspend.
Just swapped out my old pump, and this one's been a champ, quiet, easy install, and moves water fast. Really impressed with the value and build quality.

We've had these on my 10-month-old for a couple months now and they're holding up way better than I expected. She's crawling everywhere and even starting to pull up on furniture, and the soft soles still look practically new, no peeling or weird wear spots. They've survived multiple washes too, and I just air dry them. I love that they stay on her feet even when she's kicking like crazy. Definitely getting another pair when she sizes up.

I love this watch. I use the quick timer for watching the garden water and cooking. I set the alarm to remind me of places I have to be. It tells the correct time. I lasts and lasts. I had the battery replaced after a long time.

This review is for all four books out so far. Each one is short and reads like a single book broken into parts. However it's worth giving the series a try because it's awesome. The MC is a great antihero, selfish, sarcastic and funny. The characters are such that this is the most "real" feeling fantasy series I've read. Great world building and character development.
Arrived fast and packed with care. Right out of the box, the material felt soft yet sturdy. My pup took to it instantly, couldn't be happier.
This series completely hooked me from page one. The characters feel so real and the emotional depth kept me reading late into the night. Truly unforgettable storytelling.

My disc arrived a day early and was packed in a sturdy cardboard mailer with no rattling or scuffs at all, the case was pristine, no cracks or floaters. This cable technically works for charging and data, but the stiffness is a dealbreaker for me. It's so inflexible that it barely bends, making it awkward to route on my desk without pushing things around. The oversized connector housing is another headache, it won't seat properly with any of my cases, and I'm not about to remove protection just to plug in. If you need a rugged, heavy-duty cable for a workshop, maybe it's fine, but for everyday phone use it's just impractical. Two stars because it does function, barely.

I grabbed this for my small workshop after noticing dust settling on literally everything. It's surprisingly quiet, my shop vac is way louder. The remote is handy, and it really pulls fine particles out of the air fast. I'd tell any hobbyist woodworker or DIYer with a garage setup to grab one.

For the price, this was a steal. You get nonstop shootouts, chases, and a lead who's basically a ghost with a gun. Sure, some scenes stretch believability, but it's pure escapist fun that doesn't pretend to be anything else. Perfect weekend read when you just want to unplug.

I grabbed a pack of these generic card knives to stash in my wallet and hand out to a couple buddies. They're fine for what they are, a cheap novelty that actually cuts tape and opens boxes in a pinch. The steel is thin and flexes more than I'd like, so I wouldn't trust it for anything beyond light tasks. Out of the four, one had a slightly gritty fold action that never really smoothed out. The lack of branding doesn't bug me, but the overall feel is just okay. They're functional enough to keep around, but don't expect anything sturdy or long-lasting. Decent for the price, but I probably wouldn't reorder.

Beejays has this wonderful easy to use spiralizer. It helps to minimize the prep time for your food preparation. Spiralizer is sleek and durable. It is compact enough to fit in your kitchen drawers. It comes with a cleaning brush and a free rotary peeler. This peeler has three blades for various cuts. It has a smooth surface, a small size ridge maker, & a large size ridge maker. This would be great for cutting potatoes into ridged potato chips. You use firm veggies to work best in the spiralizer. I used potatoes to make a mashed potato dish; carrots, cucumbers, and sweet potatoes to make a veggie stir fry; and also made a spiralizer veggie salad with pasta and Italian dressing. They were all fantastic additions to my meals. This spiralizer also comes with a free recipe book to help you on your way to eating healthier. They have some great looking recipes I can't wait to try. I received this Beejays spiralizer set for a reduced fee for my review, however, my opinion is my own and unbiased.

I picked this up for my niece who's deep in a gimme-gimme phase, and honestly it sparked the best chat we've ever had. The artwork is gorgeous, but the message lands so gently, it's never preachy. it was ok for an hour long old time radio presentation. Don't know that I would buy it again, had I to do it over. I already have a full blown carpet steamer (Hoover brand) that works and that for the most part suits my needs for large carpet areas, but I wanted something less cumbersome for small events/spills/dog puke and such, and for stairs and tighter spaces that the larger cleaner can't easily get to. This fits the bill. It's easy to haul, store, use and clean (and sadly I have had opportunity to use it regularly!)

I went into this expecting a typical sports romance, but the dynamic between the reporter and the hockey player surprised me in a good way. The setup with the auction feels a little forced, but once they start actually talking, the story settles into a comfortable rhythm. Both leads have genuine baggage they're working through, and the dialogue doesn't rely on constant miscommunication to create tension, which I appreciated. It's not groundbreaking, but it's a solid, feel-good read with enough emotional depth to keep me turning pages. I knocked off a star because the pacing drags a bit in the middle and I wanted a little more payoff from the side characters. Still, I'd recommend it for a lazy weekend.
This cross is beautifully crafted and super comfortable. It's held up great through daily wear and always gets nice compliments.
I couldn't stream content off of the device to anything. I gave up shortly after I got it and sent it back. Not worth the money, the app and device just 'aren't' there.

The book arrived in perfect shape, no dents or scuffs despite the heavy hardcover. Tight, clean packaging really impressed me. Flipping through it for the first time, I could already tell it's going to be a memorable read.

I wasn't sure these bins would handle our massive Lego collection, but they're surprisingly sturdy and stack nicely. They easily swallow baseplates and bulky builds, which keeps my kid's floor clear. Way better than I expected for the price.
Totally worth it, the print is stunning for what I paid.

I bought this book a few years back, and recently gave it to my grandfather as a gift. He read it (and loved it) so he gave it to a friend who then ordered two copies for his friends. Then I took this new copy I bought and gave it as a gift, so now I have to buy my third copy!

I did appreciate the crisp period uniforms, costume design nailed that detail. Otherwise, it's way too preachy about honor without earning the emotion. Two hours felt like three, and I kept checking my watch.

I picked this up on a whim after hearing it referenced in a podcast, and wow, I'm so glad I did. Huxley's vision is unsettling in the best way because it doesn't rely on obvious oppression to make its point. Instead, it's all comfort, convenience, and distraction, which honestly feels way more chilling once you sit with it. The characters are memorable even when they're frustrating, and the whole mood sticks with you long after you close the book. It's one of those reads where you keep thinking about small details days later. If you want something that's entertaining but also quietly disturbing in how familiar it seems, this absolutely delivers.

These silvervine sticks arrived way faster than I expected and were packed in a simple resealable bag that keeps them fresh. When I opened it, the smell was mild and earthy, not overwhelming at all. The texture is rough enough to actually scrape her teeth while she chews, and I already notice her breath is way less funky after just a few days. Arrived super well-packed with zero damage. Unboxing felt solid and premium right away, fits perfectly in my crowded fridge door, so happy.

I saw the original film when it hit the theatres as a preview for public school history classes when I was teaching in San Antonio somewhere in early 70's. I was entranced by Christopher Plummer's rendition of the trusting Incan ruler. For its time, the film-making was spectacular. These little wireless switches are a lifesaver for my workshop setup. I installed three of them around the space and now I don't have to fumble for a pull chain in the dark. Pairing was simple enough, took maybe 30 seconds each. The range is solid, even through a metal storage cabinet and a drywall partition. I appreciate the low-profile design since it doesn't snag on things when I walk past. The tactile click feels reassuring without being loud. I did have to jot down my dip switch settings to keep things straight across multiple units, but that's just part of the initial setup. Honestly, for the price, these have made my daily routine way smoother than I expected.

A compact adapter that converts a USB C to a USB A. These very thin laptops are too small for an optical drive. Mine has two USB A ports and a USB C port. The BluRay drive requires BOTH of the USB A ports to function. Another lack on the laptop brcause of dimensions is there is no Ethernet port. My Ethernet adapter is USB A. I use the Aukey adapter to connect the Ethernet adapter to the USB C port. Next I will find out whether a USB multiple output block will run satisfactorily with several USB devices connected.

Most of the reviews I've read here below 2 stars are failing to treat this blender with care. If you are making smoothies, make sure you do the following: 1) Always put liquid like nectar into the cup FIRST - very important. It should be at least 1/3 of the contents. 2) Don't stick large pieces of fruit into the container. Cut them up into 1" or less cubes. This will make it easier to blend. 3) Do not put large ice cubes in the container. It will get stuck between the container and the blades. The blades are not big. If you push your luck too far and hit the blender button, the ice cubes will get stuck. You can burn out the motor that way, let alone break the container. 4) Do not fill the container more than 75% of the contents. That will prevent any explosion and leaking. 5) Do not press the button for long durations. Do quick presses to let the contents move around and displace itself. If you get ice cubes stuck and continue to press, you will burn out the motor. 6) Always lock the cup into the base. It is really easy to just shove it in the base and hit the button. Don't. There are icons on the cup that align with the base. Use them to avoid problems. 7) Watch the contents in the cup. If they are stuck and not moving, stop pressing the button. Take those items out and cut them into smaller sizes. 8) You will know you are pushing your luck too far when you start to smell an odor coming from the unit. Stop blending and let the motor cool down. I've made many smoothies this way and they turn out pretty good. But just don't expect professional quality because the blades and motor are nowhere near a professional/commercial quality blender.

Trying to find shoes that a) fit correctly; and b) don't cause me to limp after a few hours is nearly impossible. I have literally spent hundreds upon hundreds of dollars in the past five years trying to find footwear that I can actually wear on a day to day basis. I have tried orthopedic inserts, Dr. Scholls inserts...you name it - I've probably tried it at least once. Sandals were by far the most difficult to find, as most do not offer any support at all. This results in my feet taking a pounding and me being in excruciating pain within an hour or so. Unfortunately, it is hot and humid where I live from May to late September - my feet sweat easy - so I really do not want to wear closed shoes and have to deal with *that* particular issue. These Oofos...I'm in love. I can wear these all day, and my feet are so happy. The comfort, the arch support - phenomenal. I love the black/silver color combination - it looks very classy and goes with pretty much anything I wear. Seriously, these are more comfortable than my $200 running shoes. I can't even be barefoot anymore - when I am at home, puttering around and doing chores, I have these babies on. I put them in my work tote and slip out of my dress shoes and wear these at my desk. I simply cannot get enough of them. I will be purchasing more of these Oolala thong flip flops, in different colors. I no longer dread family outings (say, at an amusement park) because I finally have comfortable footwear to walk around in all day, without having to be the wet blanket that continually needs to sit down and "rest her feet." I wish I could give these sandals more than 5 stars. They're perfect. I am a very, very satisfied customer!

Habits we all have them, we all use them but we all don't know why or how. Yet our habits, many would say, rule us. They are powerful factors in what we do, in who we are, They influence our buying habits. guide us through complex actions such as driving a car all without the hint of a thought on our part. And that's a blessing for we could not possibly think of every detail of every task we have to do. Habits are our overdrive mechanism. This book explores the power of our habits and how we can change them in ourselves, in the work place and in society. A full forty percent of what we do every day is predicated on engrained habits. Our responses particularly to mundane activities are automatic, don't require any thinking and fall into routines, one triggering another. The process provides comfort and thus we keep repeating them. Even if the habit reinforces bad behavior, its comfort zone reinforces our behavior. The book is replete with examples of how clever people manipulated our habits or created new ones in us to have us support a particular sports team, buy Pepsodent or Febrez. All accomplished subconsciously on a hapless public. A thoughtful reading of this book could make you a more sophisticated consumer. A chapter entitled "The Golden Rule of Habit Change" provides pragmatic advice not only on discovering your hidden habits but how to intervene to change them. The author provides many examples of persons who did just that. Their stories are downright inspiring.
Totally worth every penny, hooked from page one. Fast-paced and emotional, I devoured it in a single sitting.

JoJo Moyes really knows how to make me cry. Or maybe it is just Louisa Clark that makes me cry. I don't mean sobbing heartbreaking cry the way Me Before You did but I mean like just happy soul rending grief tinged cry. I really enjoyed that the author took the time out to heal Louisa in the slow painful way of humans. Grief is hard and especially hard when you aren't left a stipend and a way to remove yourself from the things that remind you most of the departed person. I just appreciated how - human- the author made Louisa. Its not like a whole book later she was magically healed. This was sticky and messy. In many ways i doubted her just as she doubted herself. It wasn't fair of me but thats life. Clark was remarkably human and beautiful for it. Im curious as to her next adventure however i don't know if i outright want to continue with the series.if this is series. In short the author gave us a beautifully heartbroken yet mending and learning story. I am proud of Louisa and her family. I am so proud of her. I loved this.
