I've built a handful of systems over the years, so I'm not totally clueless, but this controller card just didn't click for me. My goal was to add SATA III ports to an older Gigabyte board and maybe set up a simple RAID 1 for redundancy. After a full weekend of driver juggling, BIOS tweaks, and even a fresh Windows install, I could only get the drives to show up intermittently. The RAID utility is clunky and kept freezing on me. Honestly, I'd only recommend this to someone who really enjoys troubleshooting obscure IRQ conflicts and has deep patience, maybe a vintage hardware enthusiast. For anyone wanting a straightforward upgrade without the headache, I'd say look elsewhere or stick with your motherboard's native ports.
My kitty finally stopped meowing during car rides now.
Surprisingly sturdy and way prettier in person than I expected.

You will sweat! This suit is CA Prop compliant according to the manufacturer, so I ordered. I wear dry fit close underneath, and use with my spin bike. My goal is to drop some body fat before spring break, and I believe this suit will get me there based on the last three weeks of workouts. By the way, I'm 45 yrs old, and after eating one too many scoops of ice cream, this is my aggressive but safe way to slim down.
Perfect for DIYers building a shed or deck. Hits hard, never jams, and the case keeps everything tidy.
Cuts great plugs, sharp and tapered for easy insertion. Only wish it could cut deeper plugs.

Excellent book. I loved the characters and the premise. The writing was spot on. My only problem was it was too short! I want more! Thank goodness the other two books in this series are out and winging their way to my Kindle even as I type this.

Eugene Peterson does his usual terrific job with his analysis of King David. One of the best take aways for me was his chapter on friendship as a spiritual disciple. Without Jonathan's friendship David's life may have turned out quit differently than it did. It reminded me of the importance, even the divine intention in creating people with the capacity for friendships.
My cats love this food. They gobble it up. It smells good and stays down for hours without any problems.

I put this on my 26-foot travel trailer and paired it with a wireless setup, honestly, it's a game changer. The image is clear enough that I can spot small obstacles behind me, even in direct sunlight. There's a slight delay when the camera feed kicks in, but you get used to it fast. I tow through some tight campsites and narrow forest roads, and this has taken so much stress out of backing into tricky spots. If you're someone who camps solo or just hates relying on hand signals from strangers, this is for you. I'd especially recommend it to RV owners who regularly find themselves in tight spaces or boondocking where turnarounds get sketchy.

I really wanted to like this thing. The concept is perfect for travel, ditch the laptop and back up photos straight from SD to a USB drive. In reality, it's a total letdown. The hardware feels cheap, but the real failure is the companion app. It constantly crashes mid-transfer, so bulk copying is impossible. I managed to move maybe three photos before it froze and disconnected. The whole point is hands-off batch transfers, and it simply can't handle that. It's basically useless unless you enjoy manually babysitting one file at a time. Completely fails to deliver on its core promise.

The NeverCurl is one phenomenal product! I had floors refinished and didn't want anything sticky - this solved the problem. Adheres to the underside of area rug leaving the soft pliable plastic side on the floor, so no scuff or skid marks. Also makes rug easy to move when cleaning the wood floor. Corners stay flat and curl-free too! And provides a non-accident environment for kids, pets and elder folk. A true treasure found with the NeverCurl product!
My dog took to it right away, and walks feel so much smoother now. The handle is comfy even on longer routes, and it clips on fast. Really happy with how it's held up after weeks of daily use.
For the cost, this little thing saved me a ton in vet bills. Totally worth it.

I've had this paperback stuffed in my bag for months now, through rain, coffee spills, and a few too many rereads, and it's held up shockingly well. The spine hasn't cracked, pages are still crisp. Austen purists might side-eye a murder plot, but honestly, it's so cleverly woven in that it feels like coming home to old friends. After multiple flips through my favorite scenes, the binding's still tight, no loose pages. Perfect beach read that's tougher than it looks.
Feels solid, looks great in our space, and makes the whole room feel more put together.
I was honestly expecting a standard DVD transfer but the picture is crisp, way better than I remembered it looking.
Got this for my backyard birthday party and the kids went nuts chasing the constant stream of bubbles. Simple setup and it's held up to heavy use all summer long.

Comfortable pads that fit on the outside of the ears. (not on top so it doesn't crush the ears) The mic is clear and crisp. The sound is premium. Lots of play room so it's not squeezing my head as I have ran into in the past with other headsets. GREAT product and not too expensive.

Score another hot steamy romance in the Baltimore Banners series. Mat truly is a saint, a hot as well hockey player, but a saint non the less. Mat meets Nicole in a chance encounter. Sparks fly however Nicole slips away leaving Mat wanting. Nicole is struggling to put her past behind her. Mat wants to support Nicole encouraging to chase her dreams. This story touches on real life issues while incorporating sex, love, and happily ever after!

This was definitely a short story; leaving the reader wanting a few more pages to just end the curiosity of what happens next. Would recomend this to anyone looking to read a story of calling the wrong guy and then frantically searching for him.

I went in expecting a light read, but this delivered more heart than I anticipated. The mystery thread kept me guessing, and the heroine's resilience felt real, not forced. The belly dancing angle was such a fresh, clever touch, unexpected and fun. Definitely a standout in the genre.
I loved how the final chapters wrapped up every loose thread without feeling rushed, finally, a satisfying, heartfelt conclusion.
Its an authentic Louisville Slugger what more can you ask for? Solid wood, weighted real nice.
Had this little scale for a few months now and it's held up nicely to daily coffee measuring and occasional baking. Tiny scratch on the platform but still spot-on accurate.

My copy arrived with a smashed corner and a crease running down the back cover because it was just tossed in a bubble mailer with zero padding. The book itself looks and feels cheap, the cover stock is weirdly thin and already curling after two days on my shelf. I opened it hoping the story would salvage things, but honestly the packaging fiasco killed my excitement. First few chapters felt sluggish and I had to force myself to keep going, which isn't what you want from a new paperback. Not unreadable, but definitely disappointing right out of the gate.

This commentary is solid, but the Kindle formatting is a real letdown. There's no easy way to jump between books, so finding specific passages feels like a chore. The content deserves better navigation. I'll keep it because it was cheap, but honestly I expected a smoother reading experience for something so widely recommended.
The chapter on deception completely changed how I handle office politics.

It is a good option for a beginner who does not mind spending the extra money. It is over priced for the purpose it serves if you have good wood working skills. It is a well made product 4 for quality but a 2.5 star for the price

We are taking care of an abandoned cat outdoors and I can tell you that this works perfectly! The water stays cold and there is no freezing - no ice at all. We've had quite a bitter winter this year. A lot of 1 digit and below 0 days and it works just great.
Arrived in perfect condition and the slipcover was pristine, looks amazing on my shelf.
Arrived super fast in perfect packaging. Instantly impressed by how sleek and sturdy these look right out of the box.

I've reread this a few times now and the binding's held up great, no loose pages or spine cracks. The morally grey world still hooks me, though some sections drag a bit. Solid read that stands up to repeat use.

FEELS fabulous. Down marks for the metal case making the key clicks resonate and ring too much, and that the 10-key pad is IBM standard (with double size plus key) and NOT Mac standard, which is crucial for working in ProTools.
Looks great on my gallery wall, super sturdy.

This book arrived in flawless condition, thanks to the sturdy packaging and the extra care taken to protect the corners and spine. No dings, no creases, just a crisp, clean copy that felt brand new right out of the box. Shipping was surprisingly fast, too, arriving a couple of days earlier than expected, which was a nice little bonus. From the moment I held it, the weight of the pages and the smooth matte cover gave it a premium, inviting feel. Cracking it open for the first time, I could tell immediately this was going to be one of those rare reads that pulls you in and doesn't let go. Honestly, the whole unboxing experience set the perfect tone, and I'm so glad I took a chance on this one.

I picked this up after my old dash cam finally gave out, and honestly I'm impressed. Installation was straightforward and I had it running in maybe ten minutes. The video quality caught me off guard, super sharp even in low light, and you can actually read plates without squinting. Loop recording works seamlessly, and the wide angle covers way more of the road than I expected. It's compact enough that it doesn't block my view at all, which was a nice surprise. I haven't needed to pull footage for anything serious yet, but I tested it on my computer and everything plays back smooth with clear audio. For the price, this thing punches way above its weight. Definitely a solid buy.
Had this level for a few months now and it's held up fine after several grading projects. The plastic housing survived a couple drops off the tractor too. Still accurate.

I had high hopes for the story concept, but the pacing dragged so much I struggled to stay engaged. For the price, I expected a smoother read, not something that felt like a chore. I've finished better indie titles that cost half as much. Pretty disappointing overall for what I paid.
Interesting flick, not much like the other Coen Bros movies except in its weird little twists.

I wasn't sure what to expect, but this workout genuinely surprised me. It's low-pressure yet effective, and I actually felt energized instead of worn out. Perfect for easing back into a routine after a long break. Definitely worth adding to my collection.
It was a great book. I had lots of fun reading this one. The love that you see is so wonderful!!!
Purchased many times and like these - they finally got rid of the cloth underside to the sandal which helps with the stinky foot smell that happened with the old design!
Holds gun securely, but still easy to draw. A good holster. Worth the modest price.

It reads different and helps in some of our Bible studies. It helps some different members understand the lesson different than the King James Bible. It also easy to find the book and chapters you want to read.
Great trains as usual. My son does notice that bill and Ben seem to change colors a lot. He still loves them
I paid twelve bucks to rent this season and honestly wish I'd just bought lunch instead. A couple chuckles, but mostly just uncomfortable gross-out bits that don't feel worth what I spent.
Bun was a bit flimsy but the color matched my costume perfectly.
It's a decent way to pass an evening, but the plot feels recycled and never really goes anywhere. Performances are fine, just nothing memorable. I'll probably forget I watched it within a week.
My picky senior cat turns her nose up at everything except this flavor. I'd recommend it to anyone with a fussy older kitty who needs a little spoiling.
I sat through the entire run of Angel but this is rough going. Maybe you have to have a Valley Girl mentality.

This arm totally exceeded what I expected for the price. I was honestly bracing for some wobble or sag, but once I dialed in the tension it holds my 32-inch and 27-inch displays dead steady. Setup took maybe 20 minutes, the clamp feels bombproof and the instructions were actually clear. I appreciate how smoothly the arms swing when I want to switch between sitting and standing at my desk. Cable channels built into the arms kept everything looking clean without much fuss. Compared to my old fixed stands, the extra desk real estate alone makes this a huge upgrade. If you're on the fence, just go for it.

Cooper fought through his struggles and thanks to fate he was able to support his family in a way he never thought possible. Emma struggled but kept her head up to help her dad, finding cooper was the best thing that ever happened to her. I loved there brief encounter in his neighborhood and how they reunited unintentionally.
Bought this as a last hope for my leather coat... did a great job made the coat look great and was not stiff.
Really pleased with this jacket. The material feels durable and it's comfortable right out of the box. Definitely worth picking one up for myself.
Arrived fast in perfect condition with no bent corners. The cover feels so nice and I can't wait to dive in.

I went into First Class expecting just another superhero flick, but it completely blew me away. The 1960s setting gives it this cool, retro spy vibe that sets it apart from the other X-Men movies. McAvoy and Fassbender are phenomenal together, you actually feel the weight of their friendship falling apart. The story does an incredible job of showing how Magneto became who he is, and honestly, you don't need to know anything about the comics to get sucked in. I'd hand this to anyone who loves smart action movies with real emotional stakes, especially if you enjoy origin stories that focus on character over chaos. Perfect for a movie night with friends who aren't die-hard fans yet.

Knife itself seems decent for the price, but mine arrived loose in an oversized box with zero padding. The handle had scuffed edges right out of the package, and the fire starter was rattling around separately. Not a great first impression. I get that it's cheap, but a little care with shipping would go a long way. Functional, just underwhelming.

This book surprised me in the best way. I picked it up for my niece, who's 12 and loves stories with brave girl characters, but I ended up reading the whole thing myself. The small-town Arizona setting feels so real you can almost taste the dust, and Margaret's determination to save her dad had me hooked. It's not overly complex, so younger readers can follow easily, but the emotional stakes are high enough to keep adults engaged too. I'd especially recommend it for middle schoolers who enjoy mysteries with heart, or anyone who wants a quick, satisfying read about standing up for family. Only knocking off a star because a couple side characters felt a bit flat, but overall a solid, heartfelt story.
Reese's dialogue is so sharp and natural, it feels like eavesdropping on real conversations. The banter between the leads had me grinning the whole way through. Can't wait to see what she does next.
Box was a little beat up but the holster arrived fine. Snapped it on my M&P right away and it felt snug and secure. Not fancy, just works.

Honestly didn't expect much at first, but after a couple months the difference is obvious. My picky dog actually looks forward to it, and her coat feels way silkier than I imagined. Shedding seems less crazy too. Pleasantly surprised how well this stuff works.

I should start off by saing that my speakers are pretty big and they are hanging at a odd angle. The mounts did get the job done, but not too sure on if the weight rating is as good as they say. I think they are one of the best choices in the price range.
I feel like it's overpriced for what you get. It is nice to get everything in one package but I was able to find plates of the same quality at the dollar store.

My kid was over the moon with this costume. The fabric held up through nonstop play and even a spill. The details are great, though the belt sits a little loose. Still, seeing the pure joy made it a solid buy.

Um...what can I say? This was beyond good. I never in my life thought I would have a secret crush on a vampire, but after reading this, I found myself daydreaming about one. Oh, and did I mention I'm very happily married? The best word I can think of to describe this book is "addicting." I read the almost 500 page novel in only a few days and read the following two books in a week. Then, I read this one again because I was starving to have my dose of Edward! But, I am happy to say that life does go on after "Twilight." For a while there I didn't know if I could function normally without having a daily dose of vampire yumminess. But life does go on. My kids have their mother back.

I've used gel cases in the past for tablets and phones but I wanted to try this style with the flap/stand and magnetic lock. Reasonable price made it worth the try. I've been pleasantly surprised by the usefulness. If I need another angle, I can use things like pillows that can be sandwiched between the flipped back cover and the iPad. Cover stays on well; I can carry it like a book. No flopping around at all. We shall see how it goes long term but for the price it is well worth it. Only downside I've found is that two horizontal lines form on the glass after removing the cover if it's been closed for a while. Can't notice any issues when using the iPad, but just wanted to mention that minor drawback to an otherwise great value item.

The book arrived in a snug mailer with zero damage, crisp corners and no scuffs, which I always appreciate. Right from the first few pages, I was pulled into Salt's world. The Atlanta atmosphere feels so lived-in and gritty, and the characters already have me hooked. Honestly, I stayed up way too late reading. Can't wait for more.
The scene where the protagonist hides in the crawlspace had me holding my breath. Creepy, claustrophobic, and brilliantly tense. Lost a star because the ending felt a bit rushed.
Really big size. elegant, strong and very beautiful.
This is one of the best authors in the world and everyone should read her novels. I recommend the Outlander Series you will love it!

We've had a couple of Chamberlain garage door openers for several years now, and they work just fine. I bought this remote as a replacement for one that was lost. I've had to replace the battery in one of the original remotes. It's a bit difficult to get the case open to replace the battery, but they last for around 5 years which seems pretty reasonable. The only indication that the battery was failing was a reduction in the operating range of the remote. Programming the remote is very simple (directions are provided with the unit), and it's nice to be able to use several remotes with both garage doors. The removable clip is nice, too, allowing you to carry the remote in a pocket with no sharp edges. Just remember: if you put the remote in your motorcycle jacket, zip it up before you go for a ride (see previous comment about a lost unit).

I wasn't sure what to expect since my cat can be ridiculously picky, but she actually licks this right off my finger. I bought it hoping to help with occasional hairballs and was pleasantly surprised when it also seemed to ease her digestion overall, less straining in the litter box, which was a huge relief. The texture is smooth without being greasy, and it doesn't have that weird chemical smell some pastes do. I'd read mixed things about similar products, but this one exceeded my expectations on both quality and palatability. She's a long-haired tabby and used to hack up fur balls a couple times a week; now it's rare. Definitely keeping this in our routine.
Does not do a very good job; difficult to use.

My 4-year-old is all about swinging right now, so I grabbed this to replace a worn-out toddler seat. The build feels sturdy enough for a kid her size, and I like that the plastic has a little give so it doesn't get scorching hot in the sun like some hard seats do. The weight limit claim is generous, and I've sat in it briefly to test without any cracking or bending, which is reassuring. My only small gripe is the rope holes are slightly rough-edged, I ran some sandpaper over them so they wouldn't fray our new ropes over time. For the price, it's a solid everyday swing and my daughter loves that it's "like the big kids."
A fascinating journey through art and history. A book that I will go back to.

So far (less than a month) it is working fine. It pretty well matches a new NiCad. It lasts for one and a half tanks of water. I am a little disappointed because I was expecting at least enough capacity for two runs before recharging it. Anyway, it will still be worth it if it lasts at least two or three years. I'll update then.
Good read, good storyline, good book to make people think. The characters learn to grow and make you think. Worth the read.

I do! The right size, the right features, excellent quality. The knife itself is solid and is not threatening in a tactical knife sense. People are fascinated by this whenever I produce it from my pocket for use. The unique appaerance of the blade is really quite cool, and also as you know (I hope) quite a functional plus. It is light enough and has a profile slim enough to sit in the pocket unobtrusively. I like the fact that you can open it via a flipper motion or by the thumb stud. I also found the same knife with a black handle and with the Damascus steel blade because I like this one so much.

Showed up two days early in a sturdy box with dense foam padding. Popped it in and posted right away, cool temps, silent operation. First boot was smooth and snappy. Honestly didn't expect server-grade hardware to feel this at home in my gaming rig. Zero complaints so far.

Love the book! The writer definitely didn't spend a whole lot of time on the back stories...straight to the neaty griddy! Read it in one day! Could not put the book down! The sex scenes were absolutely INCREDIBLE! The connections between the two of them was great!

I grabbed a 4-pack of these dimmers to finally get our living room lighting under control, and I'm seriously impressed for what they cost. Install took maybe 15 minutes per switch once I got the hang of it, and they paired with our smart setup without any drama. The dimming is smooth all the way down to a warm glow, and scheduling through the app just works. I was a little worried the plastic would feel flimsy at this price point, but honestly they click fine and have held up to daily use with two kids hammering them constantly. For the money, getting this many smart switches with solid connectivity and no hub required feels like a steal. Already ordered another set for the basement.

THE GRAY MAN by Mark Greany is a rollicking good time. The first book in a successful series. Court Gentry is a former America agent who now works as an independent assassin. This guy is one bad dude and this first book sets it up. Improbable, yell, ye, but oh so much fun if you can get past that. Action. Actio. Action.

My son's graduation gift was this chunky watch, and I'll admit I was nervous when I saw the box, it's substantial. But man, does it deliver. He's got average wrists but wears it daily without a single complaint. The silicone band sits snug, no pinching or sliding, and the face is crisp even in direct sun. He's banged it against doorframes, dunked it in the pool, and it still looks fresh out of the packaging. The bold design actually elevates his casual tees and hoodies effortlessly. For the price, it feels like a steal. He grins every time someone asks about it.
Spring worked perfect to replace broken one. If you are not mechanically inclined, leave it to a professional.

Totally worth every penny. My nephew and I both devoured it, he was all over the retro pop culture bits, while I got sucked into the slick near-future tech. Two completely different reads in one book, which is a steal at this price. Easily a new household favorite.

I really like the games by this company (Gamewright). They have a whole series of short easy to play games and this is one of them. My four year old has been able to play this because it is easy and short and is over before his attention span is over. My eight year old has enjoyed this game and others like it by this company. I'm not sure how educational it is, but I will say that it does help younger children with visual discrimination which is a prereading skill. Because this is a card game it is somewhat prone to being destroyed by young hands. However, the cards are larger than normal playing cards and very sturdy. I would not hesitate to buy this game again. Enjoy.
This is so much fun for the money! Works great and is hours and hours of fun. I'm getting another one so 5 or more can have a MLP party on rainy summer days.
Used two of these in an SLI with good results.
Find are great. Snorkel is good mask is ok. Mask leaks and snorkel not really positioned well and easily takes on water.
Rips them a new one. Great blade for the price.

I thought this DVD was exceptional. "The Frontier House series tells the story of these three families. Our cast of characters (or actual people in this case) includes the Clune family, the Glenn family and the Brooks family. The Clunes are, essentially, rich people (two adults and four children) from Malibu who live in a mansion. The Glenns (two adults and two children) are a middle class, church going family from Tennessee. The Brooks are something of an evolving family. They start out as a young man and his father, and become a young man and his new bride two months into the project. The families are very different in background and personality, making for some very interesting, not to mention entertaining, community interaction." (from epinions) Thank goodness, we were not born during this time. Life was difficult and if you did not chop enough wood for the coming winter, plant enough food for your root cellar, store enough food for your cattle and chickens and build shelter for them............you were not going to make it on the FRONTIER. This is exceptional viewing, especially for todays children, to see what it was like to survive in those days on the Frontier. Let's all be thankful for modern day conveniences.......ALL OF THEM. A good DVD to buy for your library, I recommend it.
It's held up well over a few months of daily use, just a bit lightweight.
Perfect for any dog with a sensitive stomach or itchy skin.
Use this when camping. Is a good knife for the cost - sheath is a little flimsy, and I wouldn't trust my life to the survival pack - but its not bad at all.
If I could I would give it more stars for ingenuity and thought put in to it. It's only 8 volts so not sure what to expect. Have not tried a long screw yet.

I bought this for my dog and he likes it very much, which says a lot because he is usually a picky dog. This food actually looks similar to corned beef, minus the white colored fat, this just has a spoonful of congealed juices at the bottom of the can. I did think it would have more gravy in it given its sold as a gravy cravers dog food but that's alright, I can add just a little warm water for gravy. Its mostly a smooth pate of food, except the Filet Mignon, that has slight chunks along with the pate. This doesn't smell nasty, it actually does smell quite good. Similar to canned beef stew. All and all, this is a very good canned food for the price. Its also nice that I can have it shipped to my home with prime. Another thing, I wanted to mention, I've seen the reviews others have left saying their dog had gastrointestinal upsets, mine did not, I think it does vary from dog to dog, but I've not had any issues. Edited 11/07/2015 I bought several cases since my original review because my dog loves this food and its so easy to just have this delivered to my home. I'm happy, my dog's happy and my cat's are happy they have a box to play in. Edited Again, 01/30/2016 Been buying this regularly as I cannot find this exact case for sale in local shops. My dog really enjoys this food and it mixes easily with dry food.
Powerful. I use it everyday. It's very industrial so it should work for years to come.

I went into this one with high hopes and Jennifer Ashley totally delivered. It's lighter than some of her other series but still has that sharp dialogue and world-building I love. The humor landed perfectly for me, and the characters felt fresh without trying too hard. Honestly, I enjoyed it even more than I expected, perfect escape reading after a long week. Already pre-ordered the next one.

I've been using this little recorder almost daily for a few months now. It's survived a couple drops and still works perfectly. The metal body feels solid, not flimsy. Battery life holds up well too. Honestly, it's way tougher than I expected for the size and price.
Terrific and easy to use thermostat. I really like the touch screen functions. Very easy to set up and use and change if needed. Has worked flawlessly for several years.
Snug lockup on my 627 V-Comp, really cleans up the look.

I grabbed this guy during a sale, and for the price I paid I'm honestly pretty happy. The paint apps are clean and the accessories are a nice bonus you don't always get at this range. Yes, the head sculpt runs a little undersized compared to other figures in the line, but it's not a dealbreaker once he's posed on the shelf. The torso joints are a bit wobbly out of the box, though a drop of floor polish tightened things right up. There are some visible gaps around the waist and shoulders if you look close, but in a casual display you barely notice. If you're after a fun, budget-friendly addition and don't mind tiny tweaks, this is solid.

Just got this for my mom who's recovering from hip surgery and she's already hooked. She can follow every move without ever leaving her spot, which was my main concern. Tracy's calm, encouraging style really keeps her motivated. I've even joined in a few times when my lower back is acting up. Honestly, I'd suggest this to anyone with limited mobility, seniors, or folks easing back into fitness after an injury. It's gentle but you absolutely feel it working.

Box showed up half crushed but the tool itself looked fine at first glance. Tried it on one slow drain and it snagged something, felt flimsy. Probably works okay for light clogs but the packaging didn't inspire confidence and I'm already nervous about it breaking.

The short stories of "The Academy" & "Third Watch" are best read before any of the Tracy Crosswhite novels. Both are short introductions to the character and explain why she decided to give up her profession to be a cop. Both are only 45-47 pages, yet both have a good deal packed into a short space. Each short stands alone as Mr. Dugoni carries a separate plot in each. Together, they make for a novel and complete introduction to the main and supporting characters. "Academy" should be read first, then "Third Watch.
Classic film! My Grandmothers favorite now one of mine! Came quick and packed well!

This timer does the basic job, but for the price I expected a lot less hassle. The instructions are confusing, and like many others, I ended up with an ugly gap between the switch plate and the unit itself. I had to fiddle with it for way too long and still couldn't get it to sit flush. Honestly, for what they charge, the plastic feels cheap and the dial isn't very smooth. I've installed timers that cost less and didn't have these fitment issues. It's functional, so two stars, but the value just isn't there. I'm already half-expecting to replace it sooner than I'd like. Disappointing.
Lasted three days before the seams started splitting. Total waste of money for a rabbit harness.

This stacking toy was an unexpected hit at our house. My little one got it around 8 months and from day one it was pure entertainment. She'd giggle like crazy watching the pieces tumble and now at 20 months she's obsessed with nesting them inside each other. It's simple but somehow never gets old. We've brought it on trips, to restaurants, you name it. The plastic is sturdy and cleans up easily. Hands down the most played-with toy in our bin. We skipped the stickers too.
The mat is soft and nice. It is not plush as it suggests in in the description. This might work better for smaller dogs but my 78 pound labradoodle needs more cushion.
This took my edges to a whole new level a must have!!
This is an amazing book and would be helpful to anyone who has ADD or has family members with ADD. I definitely recommend it!
I'd hand this to anyone who loves layered fantasy with heart, teens, my book club, even my skeptical brother. Can't wait for the next one.

This item was right in my price range to being practicing with reflectors and diffusers. The only reason I didn't give 5 stars is because I question how long it's gonna last. It appears to be made similar to a sun shade you'd put in your car windshield to keep the heat down, and I'd imagine the metal will bend out of shape sooner rather than later. This observation was made on more than one review, but a few people have reported back saying it's going strong. Bottom line is that even if it does bend out of shape, I can buy 10 more at the same price as one of the higher end kits... so this seems like the best investment. I would recommend this product!

We are over 70 years old and these lifts make kayaking possible. Without these, there is no way to get the kayaks on top of the car anymore. They work totally as advertised. You have to have the Malone J-racks to use them but it's worth the extra purchase.

The Rockler featherboards are the best that I have found. The plastic has a good spring to it and the hardware is very good quality. I have two of these fence-boards and also a miter slot board. Love 'em. The 5/16" t-bolts that these come with this work well in the fence slots I made using a slot-cutting bit from Lee-Valley/Veritas. I should mention that they do not fit in the slots on my Ridgid table saw fence, which uses 1/4" slots. If I want to use these on my table say, I will need some 1/4" bolts. No big deal. I grabbed this little converter to hook up an older soundbar to a newer TV that only has optical out. For the price, it's honestly hard to beat. The build feels a bit lightweight and the included USB cable is short, but at this cost I'm not complaining. It does exactly what it promises without fuss. If you need a cheap way to bridge old and new audio gear, this gets the job done solidly.

The shapes in Tetris are also known as polyominoes. The Poly Pages site by Andrew Clarke has many constructions for various polyforms. The free program Burr Tools can let you explore more. The puzzles in Doodle Fit use "fixed" polyominoes, which cannot be rotated. This helps the enjoyability of these puzzles, since logic will often force a few of the pieces, and then the rest can be placed. In each puzzle, a goal is given, and pieces that can make the goal. The 5-way is used to place each piece. Some semi-crazy black and white pattern shading is used on each piece. There are 27 shapes in the beginners pack. I got the first 17 very quickly, then had to think for awhile on #18. The higher level packs are locked until you solve earlier packs. Beginner's Pack -- 27 puzzles Letters Pack -- 26 puzzles Mixin' Things -- 32 puzzles Geometry pack -- 25 puzzles Winter Pack -- 23 puzzles Electronic Games -- 36 puzzles Valentines Day -- 30 puzzles Greek Alphabet -- 24 puzzles Nature Pack -- 35 puzzles Well done, and highly recommended.

Despite having the entire Divergent series ruined by the utter mind-rape that was Allegiant, which was in my opinion just a 526-page plot hole of nonsense, I decided to read the Four collection -- mainly because the e-book was available for free at my library and the other books I wanted to read weren't ready yet and I had time to kill. My conclusion after reading all of the short stories is. meh. Roth seems more capable of writing a boy who doesn't sound like a teenage girl when she isn't trying to write the girl character at the same time - I'm looking at you, Allegiant - but the writing style is still not all that different. As for the stories themselves, knowing the truth behind everything as revealed in Allegiant made it hard to care about anything or take any of it seriously, so I was forced to make a very conscious effort to block that book out of my mind (moreso than I already have) in order to just accept these stories for what they were in the "original" Divergent universe. And the stories were just okay. While we do learn some new details about Tobias's early Dauntless life and answer some unanswered questions, not much of it is all that interesting, and the last short story, which covers a good half of the plot from Divergent, is basically just fan fiction. All in all it was just. okay. Altogether the collection was a three-star read. There was nothing particularly offensive about it, but nothing all that interesting, either. If you must read it, borrow it from a friend or get it at the library. It's really not worth taking up permanent residence on your bookshelf or Kindle library. (Oh, and if you're going to buy it in e-book format, buy them separately. Amazon seems to be taking advantage of the fact that some people may not be observant/bright enough to notice that it's actually more expensive when you buy the bundle than if you just bought them for $1. 99 each.

I have 6 cats and this is the most popular treat yet. I have to be careful to be fair and divert the more agressive cats or they will fight over them. My old cat is the one with the bad coat but it has been improving very well with these. She also gets a joint supplement one. Her coat is filling out nicely and she is looking a lot better, much less ragged. These don't bother any of my cats, even my puke queen who throws up with every new thing. I've had NO episodes of tummy upset with any of the cats. They are little fish shaped treats, brown, and feel moist to the touch. I would suggest having another bag of treats nearby that they can have extras of, because they want more than one. They are only supposed to eat one treat each a day, but my cats got in my desk drawer and drug the treat bags out. This one was the one they wanted- they chewed a HOLE in it and got a few of the treats out. They were all ok though. They didn't get the whole bag, but just a few, I guess 5 or 6 maybe they managed to chew out of the bag, which is a heavy plasticy-foil bag with a zip top. The top held well so they just tried to get in the bag. IF the bag had been paper or a bag like party treats came in, I've no doubt they would have ate the whole bag. I keep them in the fridge now, because they OPENED that drawer to get to them.

This was a fun, clever book. I was leery of an indie title, but this one was better written/edited than many. This book gives you the perspective of a group of people playing a dungeons-and-dragons-type game, as well as the characters in the game (the NPCs, or non-player-characters). I'll admit as I was reading the book, I at first thought he spent too much time in the characters and I wanted him to "pull back out" to show the people playing the game; but in hindsight, he balanced it just right. He wrote it in such a way that keeps the reader guessing to figure out which characters are truly NPCs and which ones are being "played" by the people in the real world. The characters were all well-developed. The writing style was simple and easy to read, sometimes feeling much like a D&D module, but adding in enough of the feelings and motivations of the characters to make them feel more real. I would recommend this book, especially to those who have ever played D&D.

This is a comparison of 3 travel mugs I own: Contigo Snapseal, Contigo Autoseal, Thermos King. I only use for hot coffee. Temperature: The Autoseal is king hereit holds the temperature ridiculously well, due to the combination of the lock on top and the finger button on the side. Its a clear 5 stars. King is second, with Snapseal right behind the King. Size: The Snapseal holds more, without being cumbersome, so if the 20vs16 is important to you, this is a bonus. The King is better than the Autoseal for one reason to me- it fits under the 3 Keurigs I regularly use. Use: The King is indeed king herethe slide switch is easy, and the deeper lid means spills are captured easily. Also, you can slide it open and then drink at your leisure, without holding a button downand it comes in handle and non-handle versions. The Snapseal is a 4 star herefor the first 1/3 of the cup, its smooth, but after that, it tends to spill a little, and no button required here either (plus it has a nice side grip). The Autoseal is a 2-star herethere is almost no room for spillback, and you have to push in a button every time to release a liquid (hence why it probably keeps liquids hotter). Cleaning: All are pretty easy to clean, although I get the best warm-and-fuzzy from the Autoseal, since the bottom part snaps loose on a hinge to allow the suds to get into more parts. Bottom line- if temperature is your thing, and you're ok with a button and some small spills, go with Autoseal. If fitting under a Keurig and a good balance of temperature and use are your thing, go with Snapseal. If ease of drinking with good temperature control is your thing, go with the King. Or do like me and have all 3!

It's refreshing how it trusts the original material instead of padding the runtime with songs or pointless subplots. The dialogue crackles, the costumes are stunning, and it holds up shockingly well. If you've only seen later versions, do yourself a favor and see how it's supposed to be done.

In comedy, TIMING IS EVERYTHING. Subject knowledge comes in a close second. George Burns' decades of entertainment experience allowed him to master both both aspects of comedy. Whether working alone or with a partner (Gracie Allen, Walter Matthau, Bob Hope, Jack Benny, Fred Astaire, Brooke Shields, etc...) George often slipped bits of wisdom in with his punch lines. Sometimes those were the punch lines. I could say he was at his best with this collection of TV Specials, but the truth is he was at his best each decade since he partnered with Gracie in 1923. We can only be thankful George continued working after Gracie's passing in 1964. The show did go on, and these shows can be enjoyed many, many times - good jokes, no matter how old, never get stale.

Judging by the Cover. By Dawn Doyle 4 Stars The say blood is thicker than water, and family comes before anything. I found out real quick that blood is not thicker than Money. Everything I thought I knew had turned upside down in the blink of an eye. Demi who escapes after witnessing several family members being murdered. She is placed into protective custody and moved across country. Demi has to keep herself hidden, not recognisable while out and about. So she dresses in goth clothes and dyes her hair. Jordan the guy that lives next door is furious that someone has moved into his best friends house. Demi and Jordan's first meeting did not go well at all. He knows he is being irrational about this but he is too hurt to care about anything but his own feelings. Even with time they still cannot stand each other. But Jordan senses that there's something different about the goth looking girl. As their friendship develops secrets come to light and they understand each other better. They must prepare to protect themselves to survive. This was a very exciting book with lots of sitting on the edge of your seat biting your nail moments. The banter between Demi and Jordan was hilarious as much as heart warming. Can these two find love before the bad guys find Demi. The author did a great job with the story and keeps you guessing throughout the book.

It isn't easy for Zac, coming of age in a small Missouri town in the 1960s. People think hes slow. Thats not true, but he would rather they thought that than know the truth. While the other boys are discovering the charms of the opposite sex, Zac realizes he has it bad for a slightly older neighbor boy, Rory. Zac escapes into his own world of drawings and fantasies. Art of the Heart is a bittersweet coming of age story. In just a few pages, the author captures the pathos of growing up in a small town knowing you're different than everyone else, and dealing with that first crush which is made tougher by being unrequited. Dont be fooled by the subject or plot into thinking this is some sappy Young Adult (YA) story. Art of the Heart is a story mature adults will probably relate to more than teens or twenty-somethings. The effects of first love can only really be appreciated from some distance. Theres a maturity to the story that belies the plot line, which is not to say the characters are unbelievable. In fact, they seem quite real. Its just that they are drawn from a perspective one doesnt often see in stories about young people.

Exactly what I wanted. My old sifter was the squeeze handle type and around 52 years old. Right now using that type is difficult. This is compact and works perfectly plus you get the plastic lids.
Still holds up after several rewatches, no disc skips or case cracks.

I've been using this for early morning trail rides and honestly, it's a game changer. The beam cuts through fog and tree cover way better than I expected, and I feel way safer on unlit paths now. Mounting took maybe two minutes and it hasn't budged since, even on bumpy gravel. I'd recommend this to any commuter who rides before sunrise, or weekend warriors who don't want to cut rides short when it gets dark. Battery life has been solid too, I've done a couple of two-hour rides and it's still going strong. If you're on the fence, just grab it.

My four year old can't get enough of this book. The illustrations keep him glued, and he shouts out the vehicle names on every page. It's become our go-to bedtime pick, and I love hearing him giggle through the whole thing.
I expected the snarky dog to carry the series, but the new narrator drags everything down into preachy, joyless nonsense.

If you believe in raising your child to be a "free spirit" who can be disrespectful and poorly mannered, then don't buy this book. However, if you are a parent who wants to raise a child you can be proud of, then this is a great read. It follows the authors personal experience with raising his three daughters. Unfortunately many parents today can't be bothered teaching and modeling values and positive personal characteristics to their children. Hence, the rise in children living on the edge, crime, disrespect and just plain old poor behavior. As an educator, it is refreshing to read about a dad who sets standards of behavior. You get what you expect and what you allow you teach. I would recommend this book to parents who want to mould their children to be responsible citizens.

Box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, flimsy cardboard, no padding inside, and one of the tube connectors was already loose. Right out of the gate it felt cheap, with sharp plastic edges and a swing arm that wouldn't lock into place no matter how much I tightened it. Honestly, I barely got it set up before realizing this thing wasn't worth the hassle. Super disappointing first impression.

I absolutely LOVE this pillow cover and really enjoy having it on my bed. It's beautiful and unique and has a rich feel that makes me feel sophisticated. As a young mom, I love having this touch of class and indulgence in the one place in my house where I can truly put my feet up and relax.
My little guy enjoys the lights for a bit, but it's not a long-term entertainer. Still a decent buy.

Arrived fast in a simple but secure box, no dents. Pulled it out and it felt solid right away. The leather tip was well-shaped and ready to go. Haven't tested it hard yet, but my first impression is it's a clean little piece for close work.
So much fun for the money, keeps him busy for hours.

I love this blue version of the Petit BearGguy! I loved the mini one like this that came with the white BearGguy kit and when I saw them being sold separately in different colors I had to get them all. They are the same mold but each one comes with a jigsaw puzzle shaped base that connects with the others so you can form a combined base in a variety of configurations. Each one also comes with the attachment piece so any of the bigger BearGguy models can alternately hold hands (paws) with it if you want. Since they're all the same mold you can also swap pieces and make custom multicolor as well. Whether you use them as add-ons for other models or just want to display them alone they are really cute and very simple to build. You do not need to know Japanese to understand the instructions and these mini models would also make great starter kits for newbies just getting interested in gunpla.
My 4-year-old nephew is obsessed, and honestly, so am I. Perfect for any parent needing a guaranteed smile.
We've had this for months and it's held up to daily rough play without a single crack or break.

This one was a total surprise, lighter, goofier, and way more fun than I expected from the team behind The Artist. Jean Dujardin plays a bumbling spy so perfectly that my kids and I were cracking up the whole way through, but it's smart enough that adults won't be bored. The Cairo setting gives it a playful, old-school adventure vibe without taking itself too seriously. I'd recommend it to anyone who needs a pick-me-up movie night, especially families with tweens or teens who appreciate physical comedy. If you're into spy spoofs or just want something charming and unpretentious, grab some popcorn and give this a go.

One size fitting does not fit all! This item is definitely not what the average-sized female would consider as a 1-size fit. I am 5'7", 36 DDD and have a long torso. I started not to even bother trying it on because it looks tiny coming out of the package. Once I had it on, I found that the elastic under the bust area does reach where it needs to and the stretch of the fabric didn't make it feel like it was too small. It even reached far enough to cover my backside but wasn't long enough to wear into a public place such as a store/coffee shop, etc., after a day at the beach. The material is soft, slinky, inviting and very comfortable, even despite the fact that I thought it was too small. The colors are bright, vibrant and cheerful. Despite the fact that it was too short for my long torso, there is a generous cut to the item which gave a nice flowy feel to it. I'm keeping it to pass on to my 5'4" daughter whom I'm sure will love it! A great value at this price, especially for the right sized person.
Arrived super fast in perfect packaging. Looks gorgeous holding my cocktail salt.

I Was Looking For - 1) Large cavity satchel to carry paperwork when traveling overseas 2) locking system 3) sturdy and lightweight PROS - Locking system to keep others from getting in main compartment. Lightweight and feels like sturdy material. Strong straps with wire running through it. Nice compartments as described by company. Front pocket not very deep. It is the length and depth of a boarding pass. You can definitely fit other small objects in there as well. It works. CONS 1) was expecting the bag cavity/opening to be larger but it wasn't, disappointing. With the purse measuring 13" at THE BOTTOM of the bag, THE TOP opening measures at 9 3/4". It does not open wide enough to slip in an 8X11" paper/documents. With the top not opening wide, this makes it restrictive to access things and harder to see into the bag. I have small hands and it's still awkward getting my hands in there. 2) The lock and latch needs to be on the opposite side so it is in front of your body so you are not reaching behind you in an awkward movement to unlatch the purse. I will be wearing this bag backwards with the latch in front for easy access. 3) average plastic zipper, not a heavy duty kind of quality. OVERALL - I will be keeping the bag because I paid $25.00. It's worth that at the top end but I would not pay more then that. If I did, I would have returned it. I will use the bag as a day travel purse in crowded cities. If you are not opening it much, It will work great as a day carry bag for sure.

Liz and Landon have been together the better part of 4 years. She refers to them as peas and carrots. They go well together, but there is not a lot of excitement. They are busy, sometimes ships in the night. Then suddenly, they are laying it on the line and he proposes. The excitement is back and it it makes Liz want to find a way to keep it! She wants to be strawberries and whip cream instead of peas and carrots. And they have it, at least for now. The she says to him how about we wait until our wedding night? The bet is on. Whoever does not give in gets the honeymoon they have dreamed of. Liz is pleased even though she wants to jump Landon. Then they are busy trying to pay for the wedding, survive the in-laws, and not get evicted. Such an amazingly great story! I have not laughed, and cried so much for a book in a while. I was truly impressed and that is not so simple to do. I found the characters sincere and their quest was for the same thing we all want - to be happy we have found the right someone and have the happy after the buildup. I have to say I cannot wait for the next installment in the All About Love series. The author has really brought her A game to these books, so be prepared to enjoy with plenty of chocolate and tissues. ***This ARC provided by Netgalley.com and its publisher for an honest review only.

Got this for my husband who works outdoors constantly and beats up every piece of jewelry he owns. For under twenty bucks I wasn't expecting much, but it's held up way better than his old wedding band that cost ten times more. The camo pattern hasn't faded at all after months of sweat, dirt, and daily abuse. He says it's really comfortable too, which surprised him since he usually hates wearing rings. The included warranty card was a nice bonus I didn't even realize came with it. Honestly, if you're on the fence just grab it, solid quality that punches way above its price point.
A really, really good romance, and some mystery added in. I must admit that I figured out who did it before it was revealed. However, I always enjoy Nora Roberts' books. I look forward to them.
Original remote became unreadable after 3 years, all i had to do is put batteries and it worked great.

I've been riding on the Schwalbe Marathon Plus 700X35C for a few weeks now on my daily commute and weekend gravel paths, and it's honestly worth every penny. For the price, you're getting a tire that just laughs at glass shards and random road debris, zero flats so far, which already saves me cash on tubes. The ride feels solid and planted, even on wet pavement, and I haven't noticed any squirming. Sure, it's not the lightest option out there, but the durability and peace of mind more than balance that out. If you want a no-fuss tire that'll go the distance without draining your wallet on replacements, this one's a steal.
Really happy with this hose. Installed easily without kinking and the dark finish ended up matching my outdoor spigot perfectly. Solid flow, no leaks, great buy.

I picked this up on a whim, not expecting much for three bucks. Honestly, I'm stunned. It reads like a guided seminar from a guy who's spent decades on the water. The seasonal pattern breakdowns alone completely changed how I approach new lakes. If you're stuck in a slump or just starting out, this is a steal.
This comforter is really comfortable.. comforter=comfortable. Just as described. It's brand new looking and quality is really fine.

This book is basically a continuous of book one. It pretty much picks up where book one ended. The only difference is there is more of Bran And Tyrone's point of view through out this book along with Lance and Tide. In this book I was able to see that Lance and Tide where able to feel more comfortable in their relationship. I feel that theirs is more of a relationship and less based around the BDSM lifestyle. I feel like they practice parts of the BDSM lifestyle but they r definitely not 24/7. I feel like each day that passes Lance was more comfortable in his own skin and that Tide is really good for Lance because Lance feels like he has the freedom to be himself and enjoy his desires because Tide supports him in his choices. They really are a great couple in my opinion. Tyrone and Bran have been together for a long time in what I would definitely say is a 24/7 Dom/Sub lifestyle. They know each other's wants and needs but I feel like they have gotten complacent in their relationship and do not realize they are not meeting each other's emotional needs any longer but instead only the physical needs. Don't get me wrong they are still a very much strong couple in my opinion but I feel like maybe they are too comfortable with each other and I believe that Brans tumor scare is exactly what they needed even though I wish I could have happened in a better way. Bran and Tyrone have to reevaluate their relationship and make changes and I believe this is a very good thing we're their relationship is concerned and I have no doubt that hey will continue to be a very strong, loving couple in future books in this series. I cannot wait till the next one comes out if their is a next one that is. I enjoyed getting to know these two couples and hope to read more about them in the future.

My older terrier mix has always had stinky breath and some gum issues, and our vet suggested these as part of his daily routine. I was a little hesitant because of the cost, but honestly, they've made a real difference. His teeth look noticeably cleaner, and the doggy breath is way less offensive now. I like that they're not just empty-calorie treats but actually target tartar buildup. I'd especially recommend these to anyone with a small breed or a senior dog prone to dental trouble, it's so much easier than trying to brush every day. He thinks he's getting something special, and I'm happy knowing it's supporting his mouth health.
Had this thermometer over a year now, dropped it twice, still dead accurate. My roast chicken comes out perfect every time. Battery life's solid too.

The concept is neat with the two-sided design, but in practice it's a mixed bag for us. The colorful side is engaging and my 4-year-old assembles it without much trouble, but the black and white side just leads to tears, it's way too tricky for her age. Cardboard quality is decent and holds up okay, though the pieces are smaller than typical floor puzzles, which surprised me. I'd recommend this mainly for older preschoolers who want a challenge, or maybe families who plan to use only the colored side for now.

I've been reading the Wolvers series from the start, and book 7 honestly feels like a steal for the price. River's arc had me hooked right away, his backstory already tugged at me in earlier books, but seeing him finally take center stage was so worth the wait. Jacqueline's pacing is spot on, balancing emotional depth with the kind of growth that makes you root for him page after page. For what you pay, you're getting a full, rich experience that lingers long after you finish. No filler, just solid storytelling that ties into the wider series beautifully. If you've followed the pack this far, this one's a no-brainer, easily the best value I've grabbed on my Kindle in months.

Shank on one lock bent during first use! Otherwise, the locks seemed to work fine. Easy to program your own combination code and locks feel strong and heavy-duty. But the shank should not have bent so easily. In fact, the lock was completely open when we picked up the suitcase from baggage claim at the airport. There was no other damage to our luggage. A strong, safe lock should not be able to be bent so easily.

Doc Martin is a tremendously entertaining character on television. While he's such an abominable human being, he is a highly skilled physician and an incredibly vulnerable soul. The lively banter between characters and the comedic situations continue to amuse in season 3.
This is a lightweight pot that heats up and cools down very quickly. It is scrape proof and minimally non-stick. For the price, it is a good pot.

I was just looking at space heaters and am happy to have this one. The footprint is nice and slim, so the heater can easily be tucked away when not in use. You can rest it directly on the floor or mount it on the wall. I opted for a wall mount and found the included mounting diagram to be helpful in drilling the included brackets. I actually mounted mine sideways and it feels rock solid on the wall. The buttons feel a little tight, and I question their longevity with repeat use, however, they seem to be of nice quality so it should be fine. There's a filter on the back that can be removed for cleaning, but I can tell already it'll be a pain to get to, given the unit is mounted to the wall. As far as heating, the unit feels a little drafty, but that's how these ones are designed. I much prefer the ones that just emanate heat rather than blowing it with a fan. Overall, this one is nice and seems to be priced right given the quality build.
Been wearing mine for months and the stitching holds up great. Still looks new after plenty of washes.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up devouring it in one sitting. The push and pull between the main characters felt so genuine, and the emotional stakes had me fully invested. I loved how the story built tension around his secret without feeling melodramatic, it made the payoff really satisfying. The small-town vibe and their shared history added a nice layer of warmth. If you enjoy romance that balances heart and heat with just the right amount of angst, this one totally delivers.

Love Me Like That by Marie James is a story of two broken souls, one by betrayal and one by death. When London discovers her boyfriend is having an affair with her best friend she gets in her car and heads west towards Washington. A snowstorm and an elk causes her car to end up in a ditch. Kadin, is in his cabin contemplating how to end his life. When he finds Londons car in the ditch near his property, he assists her and takes her to his cabin. With the proximity their attraction combust, throw a baby into the mix and can they overcome any issues? Maries characters have depth and are realistic so the reader will be rooting for them to find their happily ever after. Buy it you won't be disappointed!
I honestly thought it'd be too advanced for my 3yearold, but he dove right in and played quietly for almost an hour. The pieces snap together solidly and really spark his imagination.
For the price I expected a true women's fit but these ran huge and felt cheap.
For the price, you get genuine laughs and a surprisingly heartfelt performance. Totally worth it.
I bought the dinosaur set and it's super cute but runs extremely small! I bought a size up but wish I bought 2 sizes up because I don't think my daughter will be able to wear them for very long.

My kids have been grilling up pretend feasts nonstop since this arrived. The wooden food pieces feel solid and have held up to some serious play, even when my youngest tries to gnaw on a burger. I chuckled throughout the book....love the cynicism and sarcasm. Interesting ending I wouldn't have guessed. Gave me a feel for what the war in Afghanistan must be like. Very appropriate for our times, unfortunately.
For under a hundred bucks, the crisp sound and deep bass absolutely surprised me.

The disc showed up loose inside a flimsy bubble mailer with zero padding, rattling around. When I opened it, the case was cracked along the spine and the insert was wrinkled like it had gotten damp at some point. The disc itself had a couple of faint scuffs right out of the shrink wrap. I tried it anyway and it did play, but for a brand new purchase this felt more like a used bargain bin find. Not a great first impression at all.

Jesse Kellerman seems to be getting better with each book he puts out. THE GENIUS was much better that TROUBLE, which was much better than SUNSTROKE. I enjoyed the originality of the story line and, as flawed a character as Ethan was, I really liked him a great deal. My only criticism is that the ending, while not bad, was a bit anti-climatic. I read this book while I was traveling and it really occupied those boring hours waiting in airports and on planes. A four star recommendation for this one.
Didn't think it'd hook me so fast, finished in a day. Way more emotional depth than I expected.

I wanted to like this given the subject, but for the price I expected something way more current. The core ideas are interesting if you're brand new to the topic, but it leans hard on outdated concepts like the telomere clock stuff without any real update or correction. For a budget paperback maybe I'd be less annoyed, but at this price point it just doesn't deliver good value. You can find more accurate, recent info online for free. Feels like a missed opportunity.
I don't think she can chew this one up! That's a good thing!

As someone who has worked in pastels and oils, I recently decided to take up acrylic painting. With that said, Id like to say that this book has been a Godsend to me. It has guided me seamlessly through the nuances associated with acrylic painting. I like how the author described how this differs from other types of paints. She also tells you about the different grades and all the required tools. My favorite part was the tips that she provided which were invaluable to me. This was the stupidest short story I've ever read. It's like the author just started writing every idea and rambled about it while making sex scenes.
My wife and I love them, they came on time and were easy to work with. The remote works very well and has many lighting options.

Caroline Andrews and Josh Summers were the match that I was looking for in this series. They had different reasons for needing a second shot. Love was not the only reason to need a second chance at something important. Their encountered began with the previous books of the series. Through their secondary interactions with the other leads made for a pleasurable journey for the two of them to finally make that step to creating a relationship in Forever, OR. Josh had experienced loss, confusion, and the desire to want what was once his. He knew this look when he saw it on the face of Caroline. There was this draw to Caroline that he could not deny. Finally getting that chance to make things more was the opportunity that he was looking for. Josh captivated me back a few book and the sweetness continues on. The sense of humor was on point and charming. He was all the dream qualities one wants in a man strong, sweet, patient, kind, and protective. His knack for baking pies makes him well liked. It also provides a connection for him and Caroline. I found Josh to my favorite Second Shot hero. He has my heart. Caroline was truly a match for him. Thank you Sara. Where are we heading next? **This ARC was provided via Edelweiss in exchange for an honest review.
This is a simple to use, realistic stand mixer. The quality seems high and my kids have had great fun mixing like mom and dad!
For the cost, my workbench edges look like a pro did them. So impressed.

I'm a Buffy novice. Watching only the movie last year, I had successfully avoided the Buffy phenom year after year. Perhaps it was the undue influence of a certain person that worshipped the shows as the best thing on. But it took my current boyfriend to turn me on to the Slayer and her gang of do-gooders that has made me a complete convert. I stand in the awe of Buffy. Since I have no other seasons with which to compare this first season, I must say this is a delectable, funny group of episodes. Setting the course of the show, Sarah Michelle Gellar proves her talent early on, giving Buffy the appropriate menace and high school angst. She clearly carries the show on her shoulders, yet carries that mantle with utmost professionalism. Her cohorts are equally compelling. Alyson Hannigan plays Willow, a self-confessed computer geek, with a sense of wonderful innocence. Nicholas Brendon gives Xander, a loveable loser, dignity. Anthony Head's Giles, know-it-all librarian, is stuffy and fun. The twelve episodes stand up well, with only a couple of brain scratchers in the mix. The first two set up the series and its characters very well. Some of the favorites included the "Angel" episode, that finally helps solve the mystery of the character while seemingly adding more to it, and "Nightmares" where seemingly bad dreams enter reality. One particularly odd episode was "The Pack", where hyenas seem to enter a group of students as they proceed to reign havoc over Sunnydale, but I didn't buy it. Overall, you can see the promise of this series in these early episodes. I was pleasantly surprised at the developing sense of humor that seemed to grow with each episode. I really enjoyed the comraderie between all the main characters, as they fight both those hideous Hellmouth demons and the trials and tribulations of puberty. Overall, it's been an enjoyable experience. I'm eargerly awating to watch season two on DVD now, and find out what happens to Buffy and her gang!
I'd tell any dog owner with a noise-phobic pup to grab this.
Great fit and super comfy, definitely buying more shades soon.
I have found this bible to be very helpful in its translation of the King James version. Simplifies the old English while keeping to the true content.
It helps my feet especially since we have no carpet. My feet used to hurt before until I got this cute pair.
Highly recommendable. Great piece of blueray disc. Jackie can really hit the high note flawlessly. She's amazing young operatic artist.

I've had these about two months now, wearing them mostly indoors and occasionally to grab the mail. The cushioning felt decent at first, but it's already packed down quite a bit, especially under the heel. The stitching along the sides started fraying sooner than I expected, and there's some noticeable wear around the toe area. They're still okay for quick, light use, but I wouldn't count on them holding up well long term if you're on your feet much.

I went in expecting a cute rom-com, but it completely blew me away with its charm and heart. The pacing is perfect, the cast has genuine chemistry, and it left me smiling for hours. Honestly, it exceeded every hope I had, my new go-to when I need a guaranteed mood boost.
I've watched this three times already and it still holds up, no skips, no dull moments, just pure storytelling gold.
The picture quality is nice, but the pieces come apart easily if my child bumps into them, and some of them are not exactly flat, so I have to push them down onto our flat hard wood floor.

This is a good novel for men. After getting dumped by his latest team for singlehandedly blowing the AFC chapionship game,backup quarterback Rick Dockery redeems his career in Italy-and finds love in the process. This is a lively,fast-moving story with a delightful cast of characters(the Italian players are hilarious!).Grisham nails the settings perfectly, while his love and knowledge of football come through loud and clear. It may not be art,but "Playing For Pizza" is a light,quick,and fun read.

The box arrived pretty dented, which was a bummer for gifting, but the play mat inside was fine. Packaging felt minimal and a little flimsy. First impression was that it's tinier than I pictured, but the soft mat design is a nice touch for small hands. It's okay overall.
Perfect for any kid obsessed with mystery shows, my nephew drags this Scooby everywhere now!
Arrived super fast and the bag was sealed perfectly. My cat sniffed it once and started purring immediately.

I have just purchased my second of these mixers, as my daughter "burned out" the first making cookies (snickerdoodles - not that heavy). That I am buying another is a reflection of what I like about it - it is very effective in light tasks, from whipping cream to mixing cakes. It is comfortable in my hands, sits on the counter in a stable fashion when I am scraping the bowl, and ejects the beaters easily. However it is not meant for heavy tasks - for that I have a KitchenAid stand mixer (of course). So I gave it a 4 only because it is not really powerful.
Very good book. excellent and easy reading. I would recommend this book to everyone both young and old.

I was a little worried when the box showed up looking like it had been through a wrestling match, but the mill itself was spotless inside. It's got a nice weight and the grind adjustment is smooth. Took a star off because the packaging could definitely be sturdier for transit.
as an amputee, I collect all things legless! this actually is a sturdy, working, cork screw. bought a second for a pirate loving brother!

Some may enjoy this overpriced series. I did not. These books are badly in need of an editor and a more interesting main character. Lexi Graves is shallow, often silly, and altogether a bit of a bore. Lexi also has a really hard time figuring out who the villain is until she has missed many of the clues, messed up the investigation, and gotten herself into a desperate situation that she then has to be rescued from. Although the secondary characters are well-drawn, and the plots are interesting enough, the series as a whole does not justify the price. I paid for the set, based on the good reviews, but I was having a difficult time reading past all the grammar mistakes and language oddities until I hit on the happy solution of bookmarking all the errors as if I were grading a paper. By the end of the third book I had more than 100 bookmarks.

I'm only giving 2 stars because of how annoying this toy gets. It's so loud and there is no on off or lower volume setting. As soon as the battery is in if the trigger is pulled it goes off extremely loudly. It's a good product maybe for a child old enough to play outside on there own but for my toddler the fact I could not turn it off or adjust the volume made this toy a hell on earth for me. However it does work well and lights up and blasts loudly for the price so if you don't mind the fact it's never ending, then go for it!!!!

I picked this up after a long break from McEwan's work, and I'd say it lands somewhere in the middle for me. The central moral dilemma is compelling, and the courtroom scenes have a quiet intensity that reminded me why I used to enjoy his writing. Fiona is a well-drawn character, even if her personal struggles felt a bit undercooked. Where it lost me was the later section involving the boy, the shift felt rushed and slightly implausible, and I wished the emotional fallout had more room to breathe. I'd recommend it to readers who appreciate concise literary fiction with an ethical core, especially if you don't mind a story that raises big questions without fully exploring the answers.

Nice lock. Good looks and easy to install.(If old lock installed correctly)Bought this to replace old lock that had worn out. Installed in about an hour but that included fixing a bad installation previouly. Recomend and would buy again.
I tore through this book in two days, sharp pacing, clever twists, and Virgil's dry humor kept me hooked. Easily my favorite in the series so far.

My kids and I absolutely adore how this story captures the gentle, slightly bumbling thoughtfulness of Mr. Putter. The standout for me is the way his months-long quest for the perfect gift unfolds with such quiet humor, you really feel his panic when he realizes fruitcake is actually a hit. The illustrations of his determined planning and the cozy, snow-dusted scenes make every page a treat to linger over. It's become our go-to read-aloud for December evenings, sparking chats about what makes a gift meaningful rather than flashy. A sweet, low-key holiday book that never gets old.

I wanted to like this more than I did. The story starts off with an interesting premise, but after a few weeks of picking it up and putting it down, the pacing just didn't hold up. By the halfway point, I was pushing myself to finish, and some plot threads felt flimsy the longer I sat with them. The emotional core seemed solid at first, but the more I read, the less it resonated, like a piece of furniture that looks sturdy but starts to wobble after you've used it a while. It's not a bad book, just one that didn't wear well for me over time. I'll probably pass it along.

Excellent ending to the series. Sometimes you read a group of books and get to the end and think "meh; I spent all that time reading for this?" Not the case here. Storylines are wrapped up and all the appropriate people live happily ever after. So perhaps a tad unrealistic, but this is just the kind of escapism I look for in my summer reading and the humor and great writing that are prevalent in all the books makes them a must-read and one of my highly recommended summer reads.
